jobs.title: Executive Director
jobs.description:
Executive Director RCFE

Pay Range: $145,000 - $165,000

Ivy Park at Los Gatos
is a premier senior living community located in Los Gatos, California and managed by Oakmont Management Group.

Based in Irvine, California, Oakmont Management Group is a recognized leader in senior living, operating 110+ communities under the Oakmont Senior Living and Ivy Living brands. We are the #1 operator in California and a top ten operator in the United States, serving thousands of seniors across California, Nevada, Colorado, and Hawaii. With strong momentum and continued expansion, we are actively growing into new markets.

The Executive Director
is responsible for the efficient operations and overall management and direction of the day-to-day functions of their Community in accordance with property policies and procedures, current standards, guidelines, and regulations. They will hire, evaluate, coordinate, monitor performance, schedule, and supervise staff in accordance with company policy. The Executive Director is also responsible for achieving and maintaining budgeted occupancy; achieving and maintaining budgetary compliance; professionally representing the property to the community and the Corporation, and creating a supportive and enjoyable lifestyle for the residents.

Qualifications

Completion of two (2) years of college and at least three (3) years of experience providing residential care to the elderly, or equivalent education and experience as approved by the Department of Social Services.
A valid California Residential Care Facility for the Elderly Certification or Licensed Nursing Home Executive Director
Prefer five (5) years of experience supervising and managing employees
Able to operate a personal computer and be proficient in MS Office programs (Word, Excel, etc.)
Knowledge of and/or ability to learn the theory and practice of assisted living and dementia care
Able to work with seniors and patiently interact with cognitively impaired individuals
Able to be flexible, adapt and respond to change, make decisions in stressful situations, and prioritize tasks and projects.
Able to process information and apply common sense understanding to follow and carry out written or oral instructions.
Able to analyze, solve and respond to problems or concerns.
Able to count and perform moderately complex math problems, read and understand budgets and other financial reports/statements
Able to explain ideas and communicate complex ideas to a wide audience (employees, residents, family members, governmental agencies, public, etc) both in writing and verbally
Must pass a Criminal Background check and Health Screening tests, including physical and TB Tests
For the health and safety of our team members and residents, Oakmont Management Group may require team members to vaccinate, participate in daily screening, surveillance testing, and to wear face coverings and other personal protective equipment (PPE) to prevent the spread of the COVID-19 or other communicable diseases, per regulatory guidelines.

With communities across California,
Colorado,
Hawaii, and Nevada, opportunities for career growth, relocation, and travel are significant. In addition, eligible team members may enjoy the following benefits:

Medical, Dental, and Vision benefits
Vacation, Personal Day, Sick Pay, Holidays
Complimentary Meals
Bonus Opportunities
Company Paid Life Insurance
Team Member Discount Program (LifeMart)
401(k) Savings Plan with Company Match
Recognition Programs
Student Loan Refinancing
Pet Insurance
Employee Assistance Program
Emergency Financial Assistance

Our Culture Performance defines Oakmont’s culture. We set high standards and hold ourselves accountable to them, delivering exceptional experiences for residents, families, team members, and stakeholders. We pair that commitment with a spirit of warmth, fostering joy and genuine human connection each day.

Our team is thoughtfully selected for talent, experience, and a genuine passion for serving seniors. If you take pride in your work and strive for excellence, we invite you to apply.

Oakmont Management Group is an Equal Opportunity Employer.

jobs.title: Chief Executive Officer
jobs.description:
Chief Executive Officer

CalOptima

Join Us in this Amazing Opportunity

The Team You'll Join

We are a mission driven community-based organization that serves member health with excellence and dignity, respecting the value and needs of each person. If you are ready to advance your career while making a difference, we encourage you to review and apply today and help us build healthier communities for all.

More About The Opportunity

We are hoping you will join us as a Chief Executive Officer
and help shape the future of healthcare where you'll be an integral part of our team, helping to strive for excellence while we serve our member health with dignity, respecting the value and needs of each of our members through collaboration with our providers, community partners and local stakeholders. This position has been approved to be Full Office
.

If telework is approved, you are required to work within the State of California only and if Partial Telework, also come in to the Main Office in Orange, CA, at least two (2) days per week minimum.

CalOptima Health is a County Organized Health System (COHS) and the largest insurance plan in Orange County. CalOptima Health serves approximately 800,000 members, operates a $ 4+ billion budget each year, and employs approximately 1,600 staff. Reporting to the CalOptima Health Board of Directors (10 members), the Chief Executive Officer (CEO) develops and directs the successful implementation of short and long term strategic goals and plans for CalOptima Health; provides direction and oversight of programs and functional areas of CalOptima Health through division/department directors; actively demonstrate leadership in the development of health policy affecting Medi-Cal members of Orange County and the health plans other lines of business; develops and maintains positive working relationships with members, providers, community representatives, and federal, state and county agencies on behalf of CalOptima Health; and attracts and retains the talent necessary to effectively manage CalOptima Health. Has oversight of all aspects of the CalOptima Health organization. Together, we are building a stronger, more equitable health system.

Your Contributions To The Team

95% - Works in partnership with the Board of Directors, executive leadership, and business unit heads to establish strategic objectives and develop robust metrics for tracking advancement.
Monitor performance against specific business goals and objectives, ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements and company policies.
Establishes relationships and serves as the principal contact with community leaders, providers, and regulatory agencies relative to CalOptima Health's lines of business. Utilize network of contacts to influence outcomes consistent with CalOptima Health's long-term strategies and short-term goals.
Establishes and implements the policies, procedures and practices of CalOptima Health, as directed by the Board, and in the best interests of the members served through the CalOptima Health program.
Acts as the duly authorized representative of CalOptima Health in all matters in which the Board has not formally designated some other person to act.
Thorough knowledge and application of all relevant Federal and California public agency rules and regulations.
Works in collaboration with both internal and external legal counsel to ensure regulatory compliance, adherence to the Brown Act, and the handling of all applicable legal matters that arise.
Manages and directs the operations of CalOptima Health, with sound personnel, financial, accounting and statistical information practices, such as preparation of budgets and forecasts, maintenance of proper financial and other statistical records, collection of data required by governmental and accrediting agencies and special studies and reports required for efficient operation of CalOptima Health.
Provides leadership by promoting morale and resolving conflicts and problems.
Coordinates community relations initiatives, such as public engagements and effective communication with media representatives.
Develops and maintains positive ongoing relations with local, state and federal government officials and agencies.
Informs the Board of governmental legislation, regulations and requirements of official agencies and accrediting bodies that affect the planning and operation of the facilities, services and programs sponsored by CalOptima Health, maintains appropriate liaison with governments and accrediting agencies and implements actions necessary for compliance.
Hires and fires contract and CalOptima Health staff as guided by Board decisions and employment policies.
Acts as administrator of all contracts to which CalOptima Health is a party.
Supports the Board, Committees, and Ad Hoc Committees with sufficient staff.
Sends monthly reports to the Board on the overall activities of CalOptima Health and CalOptima Health's finances and financial status, as well as pertinent federal, state and local developments that affect CalOptima Health's operations.
Maintains insurance or self-insurance to cover the physical properties and activities of CalOptima Health.
Develops, amends, promulgates and implements personnel policies for CalOptima Health.
5% - Other projects and duties as assigned.

Do You Have What the Role Requires?

Bachelor's degree or equivalent work experience required. Note: Equivalent work experience in lieu of a Bachelor's degree will be approved by HR and the hiring manager.
8 years of experience administering prepaid health delivery systems in a responsible position; or equivalent combination of education and experience required.
Extensive experience and education in health plan management and managed care dynamics, as well as business planning, financial management, public policy, and working with a governing board, are required.

You'll Stand Out More If You Possess The Following

A master's degree in health administration or a related field is preferred.
Demonstrated experience leading a large, complex organization similar in size and scope to CalOptima Health.
Demonstrated experience working with a governing board or within a public health plan.

Your Knowledge & Abilities To Bring To This Role

Develop rapport and establish and maintain effective working relationships with CalOptima Health's leadership and staff and external contacts at all levels and with diverse backgrounds.
Work independently and exercise sound judgment.
Communicate clearly and concisely, both orally and in writing.
Work a flexible schedule; available to participate in evening and weekend events.
Organize, be analytical, problem-solve and possess project management skills.
Work in a fast-paced environment and in an efficient manner.
Manage multiple projects and identify opportunities for internal and external collaboration.
Motivate and lead multi-program teams and external committees/coalitions.
Utilize computer and appropriate software (e.g., Microsoft Office: Word, Outlook, Excel, PowerPoint) and job specific applications/systems to produce correspondence, charts, spreadsheets, and/or other information applicable to the position assignment.

Your Physical Requirements (With Or Without Accommodations)

Ability to visually read information from computer screens, forms and other printed materials and information.
Ability to speak (enunciate) clearly in conversation and general communication.
Hearing ability for verbal communication/conversation/responses via telephone, telephone systems, and face-to-face interactions.
Manual dexterity for typing, writing, standing and reaching, flexibility, body movement for bending, crouching, walking, kneeling and prolonged sitting.
Lifting and moving objects, patients and/or equipment less than 10 pounds

Ways We Are Here For You

You'll enjoy competitive compensation for this role.
Our current hiring range is: Pay Grade: 330 - $560,000 - $841,500 ($269.23 - $404.5673).
The final salary offered will be based on education, job-related knowledge and experience, skills relevant to the role and internal equity among other factors.

This position is approved for Full Office (**If the position is Telework, it is eligible in California only**)
A comprehensive benefits package
CalPERS pension program and additional retirement packages.
Additional benefits and perks including:
A generous PTO program
A quality work life balance
Various wellness programs
Tuition Reimbursement
Professional development opportunities
Career development opportunities
Flexible scheduling
And the satisfaction of knowing your work directly impacts and improves healthcare access for thousands of individuals and families.

Our Work Environment

If located at the 500, 505 Building or a remote work location:

Work is typically indoors and sedentary and is subject to schedule changes and/or variable work hours, with travel as needed.
There are no harmful environmental conditions present for this job.
The noise level in this work environment is usually moderate.

If Located At PACE

Work is typically indoors in a clinical setting serving the frail and elderly.
There may be harmful or hazardous environmental conditions present for this job.
The noise level in this work environment is usually moderate to loud.

If Located In The Community

Work is typically indoors and sedentary and is subject to schedule changes and/or variable work hours, with travel as needed.
Employee will occasionally work outdoors in varied temperatures.
There may be harmful or hazardous environmental conditions present for this job.
The noise level in this work environment is usually moderate to loud.

Why Join Us?

We believe that diverse perspectives drive innovation. Each employee brings a unique perspective to the overall team and we value everyone's input and we are committed to creating an inclusive environment where you and every team member can thrive while making a meaningful impacts on our community members. Our team reflects and represents the communities we serve, and we welcome candidates from all backgrounds who share our commitment to accessible, quality healthcare.

What's Your Next Step?

All Applications will be accepted on a continuous basis until a sufficient number of qualified applications have been received. Do NOT miss out. If you want to join our team, the deadline for the first review of applications is July 28, 2026
at
9:00 PM (PST).
We are encouraging you to apply early. If you apply after the first review date, your application is not guaranteed to be considered for this recruitment. This recruitment may close at any time without notice after the first review date.

Our Commitment to You

Your application and resume will be reviewed by a dedicated recruiter to this position. If your experience matches what we need, we will reach out to you to discuss the next steps. The selection process may include, but is not limited to, a skills assessment, phone screen and interview.

If you make it through the steps above and are selected for this exciting role, you will be required to undergo a reference and a background check (to include a conviction record) and if applicable also pass a drug screening and/or a post-offer pre-employment medical examination (for specific positions) If you are an Internal CalOptima Health applicant, please apply through the internal portal on InfoNet.

We will make sure to keep you updated through each step of the process on your candidate portal. Please make sure to watch for updates on your candidate portal and you emails which will be sent to the email address you listed on your application. Please check your email, including your SPAM folder, regularly throughout the recruitment process.

CalOptima Health is an equal opportunity employer and makes all employment decisions on the basis of merit. CalOptima Health wants to have qualified employees in every job position. CalOptima Health prohibits unlawful discrimination ag

jobs.company: Frontline Source Group - Nationwide Staffing & Executive Search
jobs.title: Personal Assistant to Chief Executive Officer
jobs.description:
Frontline Source Group is partnering with a high-growth, globally recognized organization to identify an exceptional Personal Assistant to the CEO. This is not a traditional administrative role, and it is not a corporate environment. The selected candidate will serve as a trusted right hand and true extension of a dynamic, highly accomplished entrepreneur, providing seamless support across both professional operations and personal priorities as he builds a new venture with an ambitious long-term growth roadmap.

The split is roughly 70% business and 30% personal and family support. The right person is a polished, proactive professional who brings discretion, sound judgment, high energy, and a builder's mentality to everything they do. This person will not simply take tasks off a plate. They will learn the business, absorb how the principal thinks, earn increasing authority, and help move the business forward at speed.

Key Responsibilities for the Personal Assistant role:

Professional and Business Support:

Manage complex, ever-changing calendars across multiple time zones; anticipate conflicts and protect the executive's time proactively.
Serve as a full gatekeeper and trusted point of access to the executive for internal teams and external partners.
Sit in on calls, meetings, and video conferences to stay fully informed, enabling faster communication and execution on the executive's behalf.
Coordinate meetings end-to-end, ensuring all logistics, materials, and participants are aligned in advance.
Prepare briefing materials, executive itineraries, and correspondence; ensure timely and accurate follow-through on all communications.
Track action items and follow-ups across multiple workstreams to ensure nothing falls through the cracks.
Travel selectively with the executive to business meetings and events; capture key decisions and action items and drive accountability afterward.
Support conference coordination, registrations, and preparation of executive materials including bios and headshots.
Coordinate vendors and manage projects tied to business initiatives, operations, construction, and franchising activities.
Serve as a discreet and professional liaison with legal, financial, investor, and franchise contacts.
Support the executive's philanthropic commitments, including charitable board involvement and related events and communications.
Oversee expense tracking, reporting, and general business administration.
Personal and Family Support:
Coordinate household operations including vendor relationships, scheduling, and service providers.
Manage personal requests, errands, and day-to-day logistics with efficiency and care.
Plan and execute family travel and experiences with thorough itineraries and seamless logistics.
Support family scheduling and planning, including recurring family planning sessions, school and activity logistics, and milestone events such as college visits.
Anticipate personal and family needs proactively, staying steps ahead to reduce friction and keep daily life running smoothly.
Build warm, genuine relationships with the executive's family; interpersonal fit with the family is essential to success in this role.
Uphold the highest standard of discretion and confidentiality across all personal, family, and financial matters.

Team Collaboration:

Collaborate closely with executive support team members to divide responsibilities and ensure seamless execution.
Participate in daily check-ins and maintain clear, consistent communication across team workflows.
Take full ownership of assigned tasks while sustaining visibility across the broader support structure.

Candidate Profile:

Required Experience:

5-10+ years in a dedicated EA, PA, or Chief of Staff capacity supporting a senior executive, founder, or high-net-worth individual.
Proven ability to manage both professional and personal responsibilities with sound judgment and unwavering discretion.
Demonstrated experience operating with a high level of trust and access, including sensitive financial and family matters.
Experience in luxury hospitality, family office, private service, or entrepreneurial environments strongly preferred.

Core Competencies:

Exceptional organizational skills and meticulous attention to detail.
Polished written and verbal communication across all channels including text, email, and executive correspondence.
Highly responsive communication style; calls and emails are handled same-day, and urgency is treated as the norm rather than the exception.
Ability to manage competing priorities under pressure with composure and professionalism.
Tech-savvy and resourceful; proficient in Outlook and modern productivity tools with a quick adoption curve.
Proactive and solutions-oriented; anticipates needs and acts without waiting for direction.
Positive, high-energy presence; a genuine champion for the executive, the family, and the success of the business.
Professional presence and interpersonal warmth that builds trust quickly with the principal and family.

Work Style and Expectations:

Comfortable in a fast-moving entrepreneurial environment where priorities shift and the playbook is being written in real time; this is a build, not a maintain.
High availability with flexibility outside standard hours; the executive respects boundaries and does not abuse access, but responsiveness is essential.
Time is split between the office and the executive's residence, with consistent in-person presence expected.
Based in North San Diego County; valid driver's license and clean background required.
Comprehensive NDA and background and reference checks required.
Finalists can expect to meet members of the executive's family as part of the process.

Why This Opportunity:

Direct exposure to senior leadership within a high-growth, globally recognized organization.
Opportunity to build a long-term, high-impact partnership with a growth-oriented principal who extends full trust from day one.
A true seat at the table: in the room for the calls, the deals, and the decisions.
Meaningful variety, autonomy, and the satisfaction of directly enabling both professional success and family well-being.
Collaborative team culture with strong support structure.

Benefits:

Competitive base salary with performance-based bonus.
Comprehensive medical benefits package.
Market-leading compensation for top talent in this specialized hybrid space; specifics discussed with qualified candidates.

jobs.title: Associate Vice President of Development - Occidental College
jobs.description:
Another Source's client, Occidental College
,
is recruiting an Associate Vice President of Development
to join their team in Los Angeles, California. This is a hybrid position working 3 days onsite and 2 days remotely. You will also be traveling locally and nationally to connect in-person with donors.

About Occidental College

Founded in 1887, Occidental College (“Oxy”) is a nationally recognized, private liberal arts college located in the vibrant Eagle Rock neighborhood of Los Angeles. With approximately 2,000 students from across the globe, Oxy offers a distinctive combination of rigorous academic programs, small class sizes, close faculty-student collaboration, and a strong commitment to justice, equity, inclusion, and diversity. The College’s location in one of the world’s most dynamic cities provides students, faculty, and staff with unique opportunities for academic engagement, professional development, community partnerships, and cultural enrichment. Guided by its mission to provide a transformative liberal arts education, Occidental prepares students to think critically, act ethically, and make a difference in the world.

About Occidental College & This Moment in Institutional Advancement

Occidental College's Institutional Advancement division is in an exciting period of growth and momentum. Under the leadership of Vice President for Institutional Advancement, the division has been thoughtfully strengthening its fundraising strategy, refining organizational structure, and building a culture centered on collaboration, accountability, and philanthropy. This is an exceptional opportunity to join a high-performing team during a pivotal stage of its evolution.
The Associate Vice President of Development will step into an organization with a strong foundation and the opportunity to make a lasting impact. This role will help shape the next phase of frontline fundraising by coaching and developing talented gift officers, advancing strategic fundraising initiatives, and partnering closely with colleagues across Institutional Advancement to strengthen donor engagement and philanthropic success. It is an ideal opportunity for a collaborative leader who enjoys building high-performing teams, leading through change, and contributing to an ambitious vision for the future.

About this role:

The Associate Vice President of Development (AVP) is a key member of the Occidental College Institutional Advancement leadership team providing strategic oversight and management for individual giving programs for leadership, major, and planned gifts. The AVP is also an active fundraiser managing a small portfolio of generous donors and donor prospects. They will demonstrate an ability to motivate and inspire staff and keep them engaged and focused on results through effective coaching, developing clear metrics expectations, and leading by example as a strong fundraiser.

Working closely with and reporting to the Vice President for Institutional Advancement, the AVP will inspire new levels of philanthropic support aligned with the College's objectives by translating institutional priorities into effective fundraising strategies, driving progress toward ambitious revenue goals, and fostering a culture of accountability, collaboration, and strong fundraising outcomes. The AVP will oversee donor cultivation strategies and events while partnering closely with colleagues across Institutional Advancement to strengthen engagement and philanthropic support for Occidental's mission. The role also serves as a key partner in preparing the College for its next comprehensive campaign.

Anticipated salary range:
$167,100–$220,000

Scope & Impact

Frontline Fundraising Management & Effectiveness (40%)

Lead, develop, and coach a high-performing team of gift officers across major, planned, and leadership giving.
Lead and collaborate with the donor cultivation and stewardship strategist to inform cultivation, stewardship, solicitation strategies, and events.
Build a culture of accountability, collaboration, continuous improvement, and donor-centered fundraising.
Recruit, hire, onboard, and retain fundraising professionals.
Set and monitor fundraising goals, performance metrics, and accountability standards.
Conduct performance reviews and provide ongoing coaching, mentorship, and professional development.
Partner with advancement services and prospect research to strengthen prospect identification, portfolio optimization, and pipeline growth.
Support fundraising prospect management and gift administration policies and practices.
Streamline and manage gift officer work with volunteers and committees supporting initiatives including the Obama Scholars Program, Alumni Reunion Giving, and the Alumni Board of Governors.

Frontline Fundraising (40%)

Manage and actively cultivate and solicit a portfolio of 50–60 high-capacity prospects and donors, including Trustees and volunteer leaders.
Develop and execute annual goals and individualized cultivation, solicitation, and stewardship strategies.
Partner with College leadership, faculty, volunteers, and advancement colleagues to advance donor relationships.
Conduct donor visits, lead gift conversations, and manage proposal development and negotiation.
Document donor activity and solicitation in accordance with College policies and procedures.

Institutional Advancement Leadership & Campus Partnership Collaboration (20%)

Serve as a member of the Institutional Advancement senior leadership team.
Collaborate across Principal Gifts, Corporate and Foundation Relations, Alumni and Parent Engagement, Annual Giving, Advancement Services, and Strategic Engagement.
Champion Institutional Advancement priorities and goals.
Contribute to campaign strategy, annual planning, fundraising forecasts, and strategic goals.
Partner with senior leadership to align fundraising strategy with institutional priorities.
Collaborate with communications, engagement, and stewardship teams to strengthen donor outreach and recognition.
Support frontline fundraising policies and operations.
Support budget planning and monitoring.
Recommend improvements and innovations based on fundraising best practices.
Participate in key advancement events including Homecoming, Family Weekend, Day For Oxy, Alumni Reunion Weekend, and campaign events.

Qualifications

Required

Bachelor's degree.
Minimum of nine years of progressive leadership experience in fundraising with a proven track record of securing major and planned gifts.
Experience leading, supervising, and developing a metrics-driven fundraising team.
Knowledge of fundraising principles, best practices, and standards.
Proven ability to cultivate, solicit, and close gifts in the $100K–$1M range.
Experience recruiting, developing, and evaluating fundraising professionals.
Strong leadership, organizational, interpersonal, written, and verbal communication skills.
Ability to build effective relationships with senior leaders, faculty, staff, volunteers, and external stakeholders.
Experience leading change through influence and collaboration.
Commitment to diversity, equity, inclusion, and belonging.

Preferred

Master's degree.
Higher education fundraising experience.
Comprehensive campaign experience.
Experience with Salesforce CRM.

Employee Benefits at Occidental College

Occidental College is proud to offer a comprehensive and competitive benefits package designed to support the health, well-being, and professional growth of our employees. To learn more about employee benefits at Occidental College, please visit: https://www.oxy.edu/offices-services/human-resources/benefits-information?utm_source=chatgpt.com

Health & Wellness

Health, dental, and vision insurance options
Life insurance coverage
Free access to campus fitness and wellness facilities, including the swimming pool, tennis courts, gym, and weight room
On-campus wellness programs and classes, including discounted Weight Watchers

Financial & Retirement

Participation in a 403(b) retirement savings plan
Access to Glendale Area Schools Credit Union membership, including financial services such as share accounts, checking, and credit options

Learning & Resources

Library borrowing privileges and access to the Academic Commons

Perks & Discounts

10% discount at the Oxy Bookstore on most items
Discounts through the Working Advantage program for entertainment, travel, shows, and more

Equal Employment Opportunity

Occidental College is an equal opportunity employer and is committed to fostering an equitable, inclusive, and diverse community. The College complies with all applicable federal, state, and local laws governing nondiscrimination in employment and educational programs and activities. Occidental College does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, national origin, ancestry, religion, creed, sex, pregnancy, age, disability, medical condition, genetic information, marital status, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law. The College is committed to providing reasonable accommodations to qualified individuals with disabilities.

Another Source works with their clients, on a retained project basis. We are committed to building inclusive candidate pools as we partner with hiring teams. As you read through the job posting and review the list of qualifications (required and/or preferred), please do not self-select out if you feel you do not meet every qualification. No one is 100% qualified. We encourage you to apply and share your story with us.

jobs.title: Managing Director, Institutional Giving [New York]
jobs.description:
ROLE TITLE:
Managing Director, Institutional Giving

POSITION REPORTS TO:
SMD, Development

LOCATION:
Based on local fundraising portfolio - must be in the New York City & surrounding area

Priority Application Deadline: August 3, 2026. Applications received after this date will be reviewed on a rolling basis.

Hybrid- Remote position, but strongly encouraged in office once per week and for meetings and events as required.

What You’ll Do

All prospect & donor management roles on Field Fundraising teams are responsible for cultivating, stewarding, and managing a portfolio of donors in our local communities who share our commitment to ensuring that all children have access to an equitable and excellent education. As a member of the Field Fundraising team and a lead fundraiser for TFA’s New York region, you will be a part of a team-based effort charged with developing and executing on a long-term vision and strategy to achieve our development goals. In your role, you will partner with Executive Directors, regional advisory board members, and other front-line fundraisers to maximize giving, playing a key role in new donor strategy and acquisition over the next 3-5 years.

Reporting to the SMD, Development you will be charged with setting the vision and strategies to raise $2.8 million or more from a portfolio of corporate & foundation donors who have the capacity and/or typically give $10,000 or more annually. You will work directly with the Executive Director to cultivate and steward your portfolio of donors, and in some circumstances, you may also be the primary solicitor. We are looking for a specialist in institutional fundraising who - in partnership with local Executive Directors and the VP, Field Fundraising - can independently develop and execute the strategy to acquire, cultivate, retain, and diversify your portfolio of private donors.

What You’ll Be Responsible For

Private Donor Portfolio Management - 50%

Manage, cultivate, steward, and solicit (when appropriate) a portfolio of corporate & foundation donors.
Utilize outstanding communications skills to write compelling and inspiring grant proposals and reports.
Design and execute corporate partnerships and engagement programs that match programmatic need with partner priorities and expertise.
Identify, pursue, and secure new funding, sustainably growing and diversifying the corporate & foundation fundraising streams.

Development Strategy - 25%

Set vision, strategy, and plan for cultivating a portfolio of corporate & foundation donors, incorporating input from the SMD, Development and ED with the goal of maximizing revenue to the organization as a whole
Work in close partnership with the SMD and ED to build the relationships necessary to advance fundraising efforts, including with local advisory board members, and throughout the NYC philanthropic community.

Development Operations - 15%

Lead stream portfolio reviews, including moves management progress checks. Identify opportunities to strengthen pipeline, engagement, and overall portfolio health.
Consistently maintain donor, prospect, and fundraising activity information in our customer relationship management system (currently SalesForce) and leverage this information in tracking progress, monitoring gaps, and adjusting strategy and approach

Program Proximity and Case for Support - 10%

Partner with the SMD, ED, and regional colleagues to understand TFA’s vision and strategic plan for impact in New York, and translate regional priorities to a focused case for support.
Participate in organization-wide initiatives such as corps member selection, and regional engagements such as school visits, regional team meetings, and alumni induction.

Your Experience

Your areas of knowledge and expertise that matter most for this role (minimum qualifications):

Relationship Building and Management
Highly skilled relationship builder, particularly with external prospects and donors
Build and execute comprehensive relationship management plans across a portfolio of donors
Excellent written and verbal communication skills

Fundraising and Development
Specialize in best practices of development and fundraising
Track record of successfully navigating complex philanthropic landscapes to reach revenue goals
Experience stewarding a diverse portfolio of donors/funders from various funding streams, including grant writing and reporting
Leverage historical local development trends in order to set strategy and/or adjust course as necessary

Portfolio Management
Set vision and direction for a specific portfolio of local donors and independently execute on that strategy in service of maximizing revenue to Teach For America as an enterprise
Monitor progress against the portfolio to ensure continuous donor stewardship
Grant writing and reporting
Corporate partnership and engagement program management

Prior Experience

Required: At least 7+ years of related experience in development and fundraising context
Required: Track record of meeting and exceeding ambitious goals as a donor portfolio manager
Preferred: Teach For America development experience

Work Demands

Occasional weekend or evening work hours required.
Must be able to travel to engage with local donors

YOUR FUTURE TEAM

The Revenue and Development team at Teach For America inspires donors and champions to contribute to shaping the future of our country by investing in Teach For America’s work with students nationally and regionally. We aim to source investments that fuel our work and impact with a revenue growth trajectory to raise $300 million annually. This role will be an essential part of the Field Fundraising arm of the Revenue and Development team, maximizing the contributions of local donors across multiple regions of the country.

Your Compensation

The applicable salary range for each U.S.-based role is based on where the employee works and is aligned to one of 3 tiers according to a cost of labor index in that geographic area. Starting pay for the successful applicant will depend on a variety of job-related factors, which may include education, training, experience, location, business needs, or market demands. jobs.description:
TITLE
: Assistant Program Director

REPORTS

FLSA CODE

PROGRAM

Our Mission

Since 1967, Odyssey House has been providing innovative services and programs to a broad population of individuals and families struggling with substance use and mental health disorders. Odyssey House helps New Yorkers of all ages—and across all five boroughs—beat drugs and alcohol with highly individualized treatment programs. Odyssey House provides high quality, holistic treatment impacting all major life spheres, including psychological, physical, social, family, educational and spiritual in order to support personal rehabilitation, renewal and family restoration.

If that sounds different than other treatment programs, you’re right. Because Odyssey is where recovery gets real.

Major Functions

Under the direction of the Program Director, the Assistant Program Director is responsible for the delivery of all clinical treatment services in an OASAS 820 certified and funded residential services program. They ensure the quality of treatment services, the fidelity of evidence-based service delivery and timely documentation of all group and individual counseling services and support services delivered by the facility’s clinical staff. They are responsible for the supervision and overall job performance of the Clinical Directors who supervise direct care clinical staff. They are responsible for maintaining OASAS regulatory compliance and ensuring that programs operate in accordance with Odyssey House organizational standards and policies.

Specific Duties & Responsibilities

Implements, monitors and provides oversight of all clinical aspects related to the delivery of residential substance abuse services.
Provide supervision, guidance and coaching to Clinical Directors and other assigned staff.
Ensures timely completion of all clinical documentation through utilization of clinical reports.
Reviews and signs off on all treatment plans, treatment plan reviews, and discharge planning documentation.
Implements and ensures compliance with all clinical policies, procedures and protocols.
Oversees compliance with all referent communication and HRA STARS verification and reporting.
Leads 820 utilization review process through coordination and collaboration with UR Specialist.
Responsible for adjudicating client issues including processing grievances and appeals of administrative discharges.
Ensures that staff maintains accurate and confidential clinical records that comply with external monitoring agency standards and internal policy and procedures.
Prepares all proposals and reports as needed.
Assumes responsibility for program operations in the absence of the Program Director.
Provides and implements formal orientation plans to newly hired direct subordinate staff.
Conduct regular clinical and administrative meetings with staff.
Prepares written annual evaluations on staff under their supervision. Reviews evaluations of all clinical staff members.
Oversees client systems including client orientation, structure, pro-vocational activities, house GI’s, etc.
Provides ongoing training for clinical staff and participates in Odyssey House in-service trainings.
Leads clinical quality improvement activities in collaboration with Program Director.
Represents Odyssey House at community and public forums.
Other relevant duties as assigned by Program Director.

REQUIREMENTS: EDUCATION/TRAINING AND EXPERIENCE KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS AND ABILITIES

Master’s level clinical degree required.
Two (2) year of experience delivering clinical supervision preferred.
Licensure as a LMHC, LMSW, LCSW required.
Proficiency with computer operation (Microsoft Word, Excel, Electronic Health Record and Outlook programs).
Must be able to work a flexible schedule.
Completion of OASAS 30-hour clinical supervision training required.
This position will require availability as needed including on call responsibilities.

In Addition To Competitive Salaries, Odyssey House Offers

A 35-hour work week (as opposed to a 40-hour work week)
Vacation Plan and Holiday Schedule
Life Insurance
Medical Insurance (Two Plans)
Dental and Vision Insurance
Additional Insurance Coverages (hospitalization, accidental, critical illness coverage)
Long-Term & Short-Term Disability
Flexible Spending Account/Health Reimbursement Account
403(b) Plan
Corporate Counseling Associates (CCA) EAP benefit
Ability Assist Counseling Services (through The Hartford)
Commuter Benefits
Educational Assistance Programs
Special shopping discounts through ADP Marketplace and PlumBenefits
RUFit?! Fitness Program
Pet Insurance
Legal Assistance
Optum Financial Service through ConnectYourCare
Benefit Advocacy Center through Gallagher

You are passionate about the mission of CFBNJ and can enthusiastically translate this focus into an effort that effectively communicates and represents the many needs of the internal and external constituents and stakeholders supported by the CFBNJ Operations team.
What you'll do in your role:  
Lead, coach, and develop distribution center managers and team members.
Oversee receiving, storage, inventory management, and distribution operations.
Ensure compliance with food safety, quality, OSHA, USDA, Feeding America, and other regulatory requirements.
Establish and monitor KPIs to drive operational performance and accountability.
Maintain and improve SOPs, operational procedures, and workflow documentation.
Drive continuous improvement initiatives to enhance efficiency, quality, and service levels.
Partner with cross-functional teams to improve processes and resolve operational challenges.
Optimize inventory flow and inbound/outbound distribution activities.

jobs.title: Vice President & Managing Director, Digital Assets & Tokenized Technology
jobs.description:
Work Location
:

New York, New York, United States of America

Hours

40

Line Of Business

Technology Solutions

Pay Detail

$275,000 - $375,000 USD

TD is committed to providing fair and equitable compensation opportunities to all colleagues. Growth opportunities and skill development are defining features of the colleague experience at TD. Our compensation policies and practices have been designed to allow colleagues to progress through the salary range over time as they progress in their role. The base pay actually offered may vary based upon the candidate's skills and experience, job-related knowledge, geographic location, and other specific business and organizational needs.

As a candidate, you are encouraged to ask compensation related questions and have an open dialogue with your recruiter who can provide you more specific details for this role.

Job Description

This is a rare opportunity to shape the future of banking at TD, leading the development of next-generation digital asset and tokenization capabilities within TD Securities. As Vice President & Managing Director, Digital Assets & Tokenized Technology, you will define and deliver the enterprise technology strategy that unlocks new market infrastructure, drives innovation in transaction banking, and positions TD at the forefront of emerging financial ecosystems. Operating at the intersection of strategy, engineering, and regulatory leadership, this role offers significant enterprise visibility and the chance to build and scale a transformative platform with global impact. Reporting to the Vice President, Value Creation Plan Technology Lead, the successful candidate will play a critical leadership role in translating bold vision into secure, scalable, and commercially viable solutions.

The VP & MD, Digital Assets & Tokenization is s accountable for defining and delivering the enterprise technology strategy, architecture, and execution roadmap for tokenized deposits and broader digital asset capabilities across Transaction Banking & Payments.

Strategy, Architecture & Platform Ownership

Own the end-to-end enterprise technology strategy and roadmap for tokenized deposits and digital assets, linked to clear business outcomes and value realization.
Act as design authority across core components including distributed ledgers, smart contracts, wallets, integration layers, and enterprise platforms.
Define platform standards, reference architectures, and scalability pathways for enterprise adoption.

Engineering Delivery & Platform Build

Lead engineering delivery of a production-grade tokenized deposits platform, ensuring resilience, scalability, and secure SDLC practices.
Oversee integration with core banking systems (GL, deposits, payments, reconciliation) to ensure integrity between on-chain and system-of-record activity.
Establish operating model and governance frameworks to enable rapid yet controlled delivery.

Risk, Controls & Regulatory Readiness

Embed security-by-design principles, including cryptographic controls, key management, and cyber resilience.
Drive regulatory alignment across AML, sanctions, privacy, operational risk, and audit requirements.
Own smart contract governance and lifecycle controls, including standards, deployment protocols, and incident management.

Ecosystem, Vendor & Industry Leadership

Lead vendor strategy and ecosystem engagement, including due diligence, interoperability, and commercial oversight.
Represent TD in external industry forums, regulatory bodies, and GSIB collaborations to influence emerging standards.

Commercialization & Adoption

Partner with Product, Treasury, and Business leaders to define priority use cases and value propositions for tokenized deposits.
Drive client onboarding, platform enablement, and scaling of adoption across transaction banking.

Operating Model, Governance & Investment

Establish governance cadence, including architecture reviews, funding oversight, delivery transparency, and executive reporting.
Influence enterprise investment prioritization across digital assets initiatives. Ensure alignment with enterprise strategy, infrastructure, and cybersecurity standards.

Shared Responsibilities/Decision Authorities

Partner with Product, Treasury, and Business Leaders to define priority use cases, client value propositions, and commercialization strategy for tokenized deposits.
Collaborate with Risk, Compliance, and Legal to ensure controls, regulatory alignment, and policy frameworks are embedded into design and delivery.
Work jointly with Architecture, Infrastructure, and Cybersecurity teams to align on standards, platforms, and enterprise capabilities supporting digital assets
Partner with Product, Treasury, and Business Leaders to define priority use cases, client value propositions, and commercialization strategy for tokenized deposits.
Collaborate with Risk, Compliance, and Legal to ensure controls, regulatory alignment, and policy frameworks are embedded into design and delivery.
Work jointly with Architecture, Infrastructure, and Cybersecurity teams to align on standards, platforms, and enterprise capabilities supporting digital assets

Key Qualifications & Capabilities

Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics or related field
MBA or advanced technical degree preferred
15+ years in financial services technology leadership
5+ years leading large scale digital assets, blockchain or fintech platforms
Experience within GSIBs, Tier 1 investment banks, systemically regulated financial institutions, major fintech infrastructure providers
Proven track record managing global engineering organizations and multi-million-dollar technology budgets
Deep expertise in digital assets, distributed ledger technology, and enterprise platform architecture
Proven experience leading large-scale platform builds within regulated financial institutions
Strong understanding of global banking regulation, risk, and control environments for emerging technologies
Track record of executive stakeholder management and multi-entity coordination (US, Canada, TDS)
Ability to operate as both strategic architect and execution leader
Executive presence and board level communication skills
Strong regulatory engagement capability
Strategic thinking with hands on execution orientation
Crisis management and operational leadership
Ability to influence across complex matrix organizations strong people leadership and talent development

Who We Are

TD Securities offers a wide range of capital markets products and services to corporate, government, and institutional clients who choose us for our innovation, execution, and experience. With more than 6,500 professionals operating out of 40 cities across the globe, we strive to make every interaction, product and experience remarkably human and refreshingly simple. Our services include underwriting and distributing new issues, providing trusted advice and industry-leading insight, extending access to global markets, and delivering integrated transaction banking solutions. In 2023, we acquired Cowen Inc., offering our clients access to a premier U.S. equities business and highly-diverse equity research franchise, while growing our strong, diversified investment bank.

Together, we are reimagining what banking can be for our clients, colleagues and communities.

Our Total Rewards Package

Our Total Rewards package reflects the investments we make in our colleagues to help them and their families achieve their financial, physical and mental well-being goals. Total Rewards at TD includes base salary and variable compensation/incentive awards (e.g., eligibility for cash and/or equity incentive awards, generally through participation in an incentive plan) and several other key plans such as health and well-being benefits, savings and retirement programs, paid time off (including Vacation PTO, Flex PTO, and Holiday PTO), banking benefits and discounts, career development, and reward and recognition. Learn more

Additional Information

We’re delighted that you’re considering building a career with TD. Through regular development conversations, training programs, and a competitive benefits plan, we’re committed to providing the support our colleagues need to thrive both at work and at home.

Colleague Development

If you’re interested in a specific career path or are looking to build certain skills, we want to help you succeed. You’ll have regular career, development, and performance conversations with your manager, as well as access to an online learning platform and a variety of mentoring programs to help you unlock future opportunities.

If you’re passionate about helping clients and building deep, lasting relationships, TD offers diverse career paths where you can grow your expertise and make a meaningful impact.

We're committed to your success and foster a respectful workplace where diverse perspectives are valued, everyone has fair opportunities to grow, and you can unlock your full potential to achieve your career goals. Here at TD, we hire and develop the best.

Training & Onboarding

We will provide training and onboarding sessions to ensure that you’ve got everything you need to succeed in your new role.

Interview Process

We’ll reach out to candidates of interest to schedule an interview. We do our best to communicate outcomes to all applicants by email or phone call.

Accommodation

TD Bank is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, status as a protected veteran or any other characteristic protected under applicable federal, state, or local law.

If you are an applicant with a disability and need accommodations to complete the application process, please email TD Bank US Workplace Accommodations Program at USWAPTDO@td.com. Include your full name, best way to reach you and the accommodation needed to assist you with the applicant process.

jobs.title: Director of Philanthropy
jobs.description:
Reports to:
Executive Director 
Position Status:
Corpus Christi, TX |
Hybrid/Remote within South Texas |
Full-time

Position Overview & Basic Function: 

Foster Angels of South Texas (FAST) is a nonprofit organization dedicated to improving the lives of children in foster care. For more than 25 years, Foster Angels has been meeting the immediate and long-term needs of children, youth, and families connected to foster care across 19 counties in South Texas. Whether providing emergency assistance, educational support, life skills, post adoption services, or helping create moments of normalcy, our mission is simple: say “yes” when children need us most.  FAST is searching for an experienced fundraiser and relationship builder to help us write the next chapter. This is more than a fundraising position. It’s an opportunity to share our mission with the community and give them the chance to help transform the lives of children throughout South Texas. Success in this role requires an active presence in the community through donor meetings, relationship-building, sponsorship development, and collaboration with community partners.

About the Role:

The Director of Philanthropy is responsible for leading Foster Angels’ fundraising and donor engagement efforts throughout South Texas. Working closely with the Executive Director, Board of Directors, and program staff, this individual will cultivate meaningful relationships with donors, corporations, foundations, and community partners while helping diversify and grow the organization’s philanthropic support. 

This position requires someone who enjoys building relationships, connecting with people, and representing Foster Angels throughout the Coastal Bend, Rio Grande Valley, and Laredo. 

Primary Responsibilities: 

Donor Development and Stewardship

Cultivate and steward relationships with current and prospective donors, corporations, foundations, and community partners.
Proactively identify new donor and sponsorship opportunities throughout South Texas.
Develop strategies to grow annual giving, recurring gifts, major gifts and unrestricted support.
Work closely with the Executive Director on donor cultivation, solicitations, and stewardship.

Fundraising Strategy and Campaigns

Develop and manage an annual fundraising plan with clear goals, timelines, and prospect lists.
Lead fundraising strategy for the Champions of Courage Luncheon including identifying and securing a presenting sponsor.
Partner with regional staff to support sponsorships, logistics, tracking, and stewardship for the Rio Grande Valley Luncheon and other events. 
Coordinate fundraising efforts for: Coastal Bend Day of Giving, Back-to-School Shoe Drive, Holiday Gift Drive for Teens, Heart Gallery initiatives, and third-party and community fundraising campaigns.

Regional Fundraising

Work collaboratively with regional staff to expand philanthropic support throughout the Coastal Bend, Rio Grande Valley, Laredo and surrounding counties.
Build upon existing relationships in the Rio Grande Valley without duplicating the work of regional staff.
Help develop new donor and corporate relationships in Laredo and other areas where Foster Angels has fewer established connections.

Grants and Foundation Support

Research grant opportunities aligned with Foster Angels’ programs and priorities. 
Maintain a grant calendar with deadlines, reporting requirements, and award information.
Prepare grant proposals, letters of inquiry, supporting documents, and reports.
Coordinate with program and finance staff to gather accurate data, budgets, outcomes, and stories.
Maintain strong relationships with current and prospective foundation partners.

Qualifications:

A commitment to Foster Angel’s mission to help children in foster care.     
Three or more years of experience in fundraising, nonprofit development, major gifts, corporate sponsorships or a related field. Equivalent combinations of education and relevant experience will be considered.
Demonstrated success building and maintaining professional relationships.
Self motivated with the ability to prioritize responsibilities and manage time effectively.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills. 
Comfortable initiating meetings, making direct asks, and following up consistently.
Ability to work independently while remaining collaborative and accountable.
An outgoing, people-oriented personality. 
Microsoft Office & Google Suite proficiency. 

Preferred Knowledge & Skills:

Experience working in nonprofit, child welfare, education, or another mission-driven organization.
Experience securing corporate sponsorships, individual gifts, or foundation support.
Grant writing and reporting experience.
Familiarity with Bloomerang, Qgiv, or Canva.
Knowledge of the South Texas business and philanthropic community.
Bachelor’s degree or equivalent professional experience.

Other 

Background check; must have reliable transportation, valid driver’s license, and willingness to travel throughout South Texas.
Commitment to work-life balance with collaborative, knowledge sharing and supportive team dynamic 
This position may work remotely for a significant portion of the week, but regular in-person donor meetings, community events, staff collaboration, and regional travel are required. Some evening work will be necessary. 

Compensation and Benefits

Salary: Competitive salary and benefits commensurate with experience, qualifications, and demonstrated fundraising success.

Benefits include:
We believe great fundraising happens by building relationships, not by sitting behind a desk. Foster Angels offers a flexible work environment built on trust, accountability, and results while supporting a healthy work-life balance. 
Paid time off and paid holidays
Simple IRA retirement plan
Partial employer-paid health insurance
Mileage reimbursement for approved travel
Professional development opportunities
Supportive and collaborative team culture

If you're passionate about building relationships and making a lasting impact for children and families across South Texas, we’d love to hear from you:
Please submit your resume and cover letter to info@fosterangelsstx.org. Please reference “Director of Philanthropy INTEREST” in the Subject line. The deadline to apply is July 31st.

jobs.title: Chief Executive Officer (Arts & Culture / Jewish Life / Nonprofit)
jobs.description:
Washington, DC | Full-Time | Starting at $230,000

About the Role

A nationally recognized center for arts, entertainment, ideas, and Jewish life in Washington, DC, is hiring its next Chief Executive Officer. Housed in a revitalized historic synagogue dating back to 1908, the organization presents more than 200 programs annually, from talks, concerts, and comedy shows to live podcast recordings, Shabbat services, holiday celebrations, and social justice programming. Its cultural events draw audiences from across the DC region and online, offering access to preeminent thought leaders, performers, and pop culture voices. Its Jewish programming creates dynamic, non-denominational, non-membership spaces for people in their 20s and 30s to explore Jewish values and traditions on their own terms.
The CEO will serve as both the strategic vision-holder and the public face of the organization, driving business model strategy, stewarding a distinctive workplace culture, and representing the institution as a voice for arts, entertainment, ideas, and Jewish life. This is a rare leadership opportunity at a defining moment in the organization's story.

What the Role Covers

Leading and developing a high-performing executive team of five direct reports, fostering a collaborative, creative, and mission-driven culture
Stewarding the organization's distinctive culture: hardworking, welcoming, intentional, equitable, and grounded in humor and warmth
Leading an inclusive visioning and strategic planning process to set direction for the decade ahead
Driving business model conversations in partnership with the board and executive team, guiding annual balanced budgets and long-term financial strategies
Championing creative vision that supports business development, new revenue streams, sponsorship models, earned income strategies, and partnerships
Partnering closely with the board of directors on governance, reporting, recruitment, and ongoing engagement
Leading fundraising strategy and major donor stewardship in collaboration with the Chief Development Officer, with the ability to cultivate and close major gifts
Cultivating funding partnerships with foundations, corporations, individual philanthropists, and national Jewish philanthropies
Ensuring 200+ annual programs across arts, entertainment, ideas, and Jewish life are aligned with organizational values and executed with excellence
Inventing new formats and event types that position the organization as a place of originality
Serving as a compelling public spokesperson on arts, ideas, Jewish programming, community connection, and live events
Maintaining strong relationships with leaders in local and national Jewish communities, arts and culture institutions, and local DC stakeholders
Operating hands-on in a small organization while building on existing strong systems and structure

Qualifications

Proven senior leadership experience with a demonstrated passion for mentoring teams and cultivating strong, diverse team cultures
Experience in arts and culture, live events, media, or mission-driven organizations especially welcome
Deep fluency with Jewish life, culture, and the contemporary Jewish communal landscape
Track record of generating earned income and securing philanthropic support, with entrepreneurial instinct to build new revenue channels
Exceptional written and oral communication and interpersonal skills; a compelling organizational spokesperson
Outstanding emotional intelligence with the ability to navigate politically and culturally sensitive conversations with tact and care
A bridge-builder across diverse constituencies spanning age, education, socioeconomic, religious, and political backgrounds
Leads with kindness, humility, honesty, and humor
Entrepreneurial and experimental, energized by invention and willing to try things that may not work
Demonstrated commitment to equity, inclusion, and belonging
Mission-driven with deep appreciation for the intersection of culture, spirituality, and community life

Additional Details

Benefits include health, vision, and dental insurance, paid vacation and sick leave, retirement savings with matching, pre-tax transit benefit, paid holidays, parental leave, summer Fridays, and office closures during the week of July 4th, between Christmas and New Year's, and select Jewish holidays.

Visit Mediabistro for full details and application instructions.

jobs.title: Director of Development
jobs.description:
Connectly Recruiting has been exclusively engaged by the Louisiana Wildlife and Fisheries Foundation (LAWFF) to identify their inaugural Director of Development. This is a first-of-its-kind role at a 20-year-old conservation foundation — and a rare opportunity to build something from the ground up.

LAWFF is the private fundraising arm of the Louisiana Department of Wildlife and Fisheries. Its programs connect people to Louisiana's natural resources through youth archery, hunter education, whooping crane reintroduction, habitat conservation, and more. The Foundation is now at an inflection point — actively building its strategic vision, rebranding, and ready to unlock a level of corporate and major-gift fundraising it has never pursued at scale.

Louisiana's energy, industrial, and corporate communities represent an enormous, largely untapped pool of conservation-minded sponsors. The right person can unlock that potential — and put LAWFF on the map in a way it has never been before.

The ideal candidate will reside in Louisiana, or a neighboring state (MS, TX, AR) with ability to travel extensively meeting with donors and prospects statewide and regionally.

WHAT YOU'L
L DO

Build LAWFF's development function from the ground up — strategy, structure, and execution
Cultivate and close major gifts and corporate sponsorships across Louisiana and beyond
Serve as LAWFF's chief ambassador, representing the Foundation to donors, partners, and the public
Partner with board members on donor cultivation, introductions, andasks
Expand LAWFF's visibility across Louisiana's conservation, corporate, and philanthropic communities
Travel extensively (50-75%) to build relationships statewide and regionally

WHAT YOU BRING

4+ years in fundraising or development with demonstrated major gifts or corporate sponsorship success
A proven ability to build donor relationships from scratch and close significant asks
A self-starter mindset — you own your pipeline, your calendar, and your results
Genuine connection to hunting, fishing, conservation, or outdoor recreation culture
Executive presence and strong communication skills, written and verbal
Louisiana market knowledge preferred; willingness to be based in or travel regularly to Louisiana required

WHY THIS ROLE

High autonomy — the board is engaged and supportive, not micromanaging
Real access — board members with direct corporate relationships ready to open doors
Meaningful mission — LAWFF's work touches every corner of Louisiana's natural heritage
Growth trajectory — as the Foundation grows, this role grows with it

This is an overview drafted by Connectly Recruiting. Official job description will be provided.

jobs.title: President & Chief Executive Officer
jobs.description:
Why Seamen’s Bank, and Why Now?

Headquartered on the Outer Cape in Provincetown, Massachusetts and with approximately $500M in assets, Seamen’s Bank just celebrated its 175 th birthday. While we honor our past that is rich with history and success, we now look to our future with confidence and ambition. We are focused on strategically advancing our financial performance, widening and strengthening our customer base, and increasing our community impact.

Seamen’s Bank is seeking an experienced financial executive for the position of President & CEO to propel the Bank into its next stage of growth and development. This top executive will be responsible for the Bank’s overall strategic direction, financial performance, and regulatory compliance. As Seamen’s Bank is a community bank, the incoming leader will need to carefully balance maximizing profitability and asset growth with maintaining deep local relationships and serving the economic needs of the community.

MAJOR RESPONSIBILITIES & ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS

Leadership, Strategic Vision, & Execution:
In collaboration with the Board and the senior management team, develops and executes the long-term vision for the Bank and effectively communicates the vision and strategic planning throughout the Bank.
Oversee the development and implementation of the Bank’s strategic plans and objectives to ensure financial growth and stability.
Brings a thoughtful and common-sense approach to the goals and priorities of the Bank while adhering to the Bank’s mission and values.
Guides and supports the management team in operational decisions and policy development; active on various internal and external committees.
Directly manages, supports, and evaluates the performance of the senior management team.
Able to anticipate and identify developing issues facing the Bank. Proactively and strategically plans to address these issues.
Serves as a member of the Board of Trustees and is the chief liaison between the Board and members of management.
Drive and encourage discussions on decision-making surrounding policies that are established in the best long-term interests of the depositors, staff, and community.
Ability to make critical, difficult, and timely decisions, then execute and follow through to completion and delivery.
Demonstrated ability to shape and steer positive organizational culture and staff engagement.
Upholding an environment that encourages open communication, transparency, and a healthy debate of ideas. Champions change and innovation without instilling fear.

Business Performance & Operations:
The President & CEO will partner with senior management to lead and develop an organization of 75 staff to achieve strategic financial and operational goals. This future leader will ensure the smooth running of the Bank, eliminate ineffective practices, and embrace innovation and forward thinking.
Fosters a culture of excellence, ensuring strong business performance based on innovation, minimizing duplication and waste, and maximizing efficiencies.
Monitors balance sheet strategies, investment portfolio, and capital portfolio and expenditures.
Provides exemplary stewardship of Bank resources.
Demonstrates strong familiarity with technologies to support business and financial functions. 
Monitors overall financial performance and oversees decisions to drive profitability and resource allocation to steer the Bank towards its goals.
Continual review and improvement of all Bank operations, including lending, deposits, credit, other financial services, safety, and security.

Community Relations & Development:
Seamen’s Bank is a solid, long-time financial partner to Outer Cape organizations and depositors. The Bank is a leader largely due to its continued dedication to collaboration and to making impactful improvements to the local economy. To this end, the President & CEO must be both an engaging executive and an inspiring community leader.
Will serve as the primary community ambassador of the Bank with a history of community engagement.
Leadership and/or guidance on the activities of the Seamen’s Bank charitable foundation.
Agile in building and strengthening relationships with customers, business leaders, government officials, and community organizations to improve the Bank’s reputation and establish a wider market presence.
Is an articulate and visible public speaker who can communicate with a diverse audience on a broad range of pressing local issues.
A genuine interest in impacting the local economy.

Risk Identification & Mitigation:
Seamen’s Bank strives for the highest level of compliance and thoughtfully accepts a level of risk aligned with the best interest of the depositors.
A required aptitude for risk management and identifying potential issues at the Bank, within the industry, and potential impact on the community. Develops strategies and initiatives to mitigate those risks. 
Maintains an extensive, comprehensive understanding of state and federal banking laws and regulatory requirements to successfully and effectively steer the Bank through regulatory examinations and audits.
Ensures the Bank complies with federal, state, and local regulations, maintaining ethical standards and risk management practices.
Monitors all activities of the Bank including new loan requests, overdrafts, and exceptions that elevate financial risk.
Leads the Bank through financial or operational challenges, ensuring calm, effective decision-making and clear and transparent communication during crises.
Engages complex Bank matters to legal counsel and ensures best outcomes for the Bank.

ADDITIONAL RESPONSIBILITIES

Regularly communicates, advises, and collaborates with the Board regarding strategic direction, ongoing operations state, arising issues, and policy.
Facilitates cross-departmental efforts to maximize efficiencies and eliminates duplication and waste.
Serves as lead for required compliance exams and various audits.
Sets the standard for professionalism and is a role model, striving toward a culture of excellence. Inspires others to do the same.

Qualifications, Requirements, and Preferred Experience and Skills

Proven track record of excellence as an executive or senior management team member, along with strong business, financial, and regulatory expertise.
Strong leadership, problem-solving, and organizational skills, with the ability to communicate effectively with staff, board members, customers, and the public. Highly skilled at managing relationships at every level and must use sound judgement with the best interests of the Bank at the forefront.
High level of integrity, professionalism, and an unwavering commitment to high ethical standards.
Excel in open communication and can appreciate the Bank’s current state and propel it to the next level.
Experience developing and managing teams.
Minimum of 10-15+ years of progressive experience in the banking or financial services industry, with a proven ability to develop teams and accelerate goals.
Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Business Administration, Economics, or a closely related field.
Must have a valid US issued driver's license.

Physical Demands

The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met to successfully perform the essential functions and duties of this role. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.

While performing the duties of this position, an employee must have the ability to sit at a computer for long periods of time. Specific vision abilities required by this job will include close vision. Extended working hours are expected at a position of this caliber, thus, the ability and flexibility to work consecutive extended days is required.

The employee must occasionally lift and/or move up to 25 pounds. Long periods of standing and moving through community buildings are expected.

Ideal candidates will have confirmed housing on the Mid to Outer Cape.

This job description is not intended to be all-inclusive. The duties and daily activities of the President & CEO will vary, and priorities will quickly shift, depending on the needs to the Bank. Candidates should have a high tolerance for pressurized situations and thrive in a fast-past environment that is both challenging and rewarding.

Salary commensurate to experience.
Pay range: $295,000-$395,000 annually
