new_jobs.title: Director of Philanthropy
new_jobs.description:
Reports to:
Executive Director 
Position Status:
Corpus Christi, TX |
Hybrid/Remote within South Texas |
Full-time

Position Overview & Basic Function: 

Foster Angels of South Texas (FAST) is a nonprofit organization dedicated to improving the lives of children in foster care. For more than 25 years, Foster Angels has been meeting the immediate and long-term needs of children, youth, and families connected to foster care across 19 counties in South Texas. Whether providing emergency assistance, educational support, life skills, post adoption services, or helping create moments of normalcy, our mission is simple: say “yes” when children need us most.  FAST is searching for an experienced fundraiser and relationship builder to help us write the next chapter. This is more than a fundraising position. It’s an opportunity to share our mission with the community and give them the chance to help transform the lives of children throughout South Texas. Success in this role requires an active presence in the community through donor meetings, relationship-building, sponsorship development, and collaboration with community partners.

About the Role:

The Director of Philanthropy is responsible for leading Foster Angels’ fundraising and donor engagement efforts throughout South Texas. Working closely with the Executive Director, Board of Directors, and program staff, this individual will cultivate meaningful relationships with donors, corporations, foundations, and community partners while helping diversify and grow the organization’s philanthropic support. 

This position requires someone who enjoys building relationships, connecting with people, and representing Foster Angels throughout the Coastal Bend, Rio Grande Valley, and Laredo. 

Primary Responsibilities: 

Donor Development and Stewardship

Cultivate and steward relationships with current and prospective donors, corporations, foundations, and community partners.
Proactively identify new donor and sponsorship opportunities throughout South Texas.
Develop strategies to grow annual giving, recurring gifts, major gifts and unrestricted support.
Work closely with the Executive Director on donor cultivation, solicitations, and stewardship.

Fundraising Strategy and Campaigns

Develop and manage an annual fundraising plan with clear goals, timelines, and prospect lists.
Lead fundraising strategy for the Champions of Courage Luncheon including identifying and securing a presenting sponsor.
Partner with regional staff to support sponsorships, logistics, tracking, and stewardship for the Rio Grande Valley Luncheon and other events. 
Coordinate fundraising efforts for: Coastal Bend Day of Giving, Back-to-School Shoe Drive, Holiday Gift Drive for Teens, Heart Gallery initiatives, and third-party and community fundraising campaigns.

Regional Fundraising

Work collaboratively with regional staff to expand philanthropic support throughout the Coastal Bend, Rio Grande Valley, Laredo and surrounding counties.
Build upon existing relationships in the Rio Grande Valley without duplicating the work of regional staff.
Help develop new donor and corporate relationships in Laredo and other areas where Foster Angels has fewer established connections.

Grants and Foundation Support

Research grant opportunities aligned with Foster Angels’ programs and priorities. 
Maintain a grant calendar with deadlines, reporting requirements, and award information.
Prepare grant proposals, letters of inquiry, supporting documents, and reports.
Coordinate with program and finance staff to gather accurate data, budgets, outcomes, and stories.
Maintain strong relationships with current and prospective foundation partners.

Qualifications:

A commitment to Foster Angel’s mission to help children in foster care.     
Three or more years of experience in fundraising, nonprofit development, major gifts, corporate sponsorships or a related field. Equivalent combinations of education and relevant experience will be considered.
Demonstrated success building and maintaining professional relationships.
Self motivated with the ability to prioritize responsibilities and manage time effectively.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills. 
Comfortable initiating meetings, making direct asks, and following up consistently.
Ability to work independently while remaining collaborative and accountable.
An outgoing, people-oriented personality. 
Microsoft Office & Google Suite proficiency. 

Preferred Knowledge & Skills:

Experience working in nonprofit, child welfare, education, or another mission-driven organization.
Experience securing corporate sponsorships, individual gifts, or foundation support.
Grant writing and reporting experience.
Familiarity with Bloomerang, Qgiv, or Canva.
Knowledge of the South Texas business and philanthropic community.
Bachelor’s degree or equivalent professional experience.

Other 

Background check; must have reliable transportation, valid driver’s license, and willingness to travel throughout South Texas.
Commitment to work-life balance with collaborative, knowledge sharing and supportive team dynamic 
This position may work remotely for a significant portion of the week, but regular in-person donor meetings, community events, staff collaboration, and regional travel are required. Some evening work will be necessary. 

Compensation and Benefits

Salary: Competitive salary and benefits commensurate with experience, qualifications, and demonstrated fundraising success.

Benefits include:
We believe great fundraising happens by building relationships, not by sitting behind a desk. Foster Angels offers a flexible work environment built on trust, accountability, and results while supporting a healthy work-life balance. 
Paid time off and paid holidays
Simple IRA retirement plan
Partial employer-paid health insurance
Mileage reimbursement for approved travel
Professional development opportunities
Supportive and collaborative team culture

If you're passionate about building relationships and making a lasting impact for children and families across South Texas, we’d love to hear from you:
Please submit your resume and cover letter to info@fosterangelsstx.org. Please reference “Director of Philanthropy INTEREST” in the Subject line. The deadline to apply is July 31st.

new_jobs.description:
Washington, DC | Full-Time | Starting at $230,000

About the Role

A nationally recognized center for arts, entertainment, ideas, and Jewish life in Washington, DC, is hiring its next Chief Executive Officer. Housed in a revitalized historic synagogue dating back to 1908, the organization presents more than 200 programs annually, from talks, concerts, and comedy shows to live podcast recordings, Shabbat services, holiday celebrations, and social justice programming. Its cultural events draw audiences from across the DC region and online, offering access to preeminent thought leaders, performers, and pop culture voices. Its Jewish programming creates dynamic, non-denominational, non-membership spaces for people in their 20s and 30s to explore Jewish values and traditions on their own terms.
The CEO will serve as both the strategic vision-holder and the public face of the organization, driving business model strategy, stewarding a distinctive workplace culture, and representing the institution as a voice for arts, entertainment, ideas, and Jewish life. This is a rare leadership opportunity at a defining moment in the organization's story.

What the Role Covers

Leading and developing a high-performing executive team of five direct reports, fostering a collaborative, creative, and mission-driven culture
Stewarding the organization's distinctive culture: hardworking, welcoming, intentional, equitable, and grounded in humor and warmth
Leading an inclusive visioning and strategic planning process to set direction for the decade ahead
Driving business model conversations in partnership with the board and executive team, guiding annual balanced budgets and long-term financial strategies
Championing creative vision that supports business development, new revenue streams, sponsorship models, earned income strategies, and partnerships
Partnering closely with the board of directors on governance, reporting, recruitment, and ongoing engagement
Leading fundraising strategy and major donor stewardship in collaboration with the Chief Development Officer, with the ability to cultivate and close major gifts
Cultivating funding partnerships with foundations, corporations, individual philanthropists, and national Jewish philanthropies
Ensuring 200+ annual programs across arts, entertainment, ideas, and Jewish life are aligned with organizational values and executed with excellence
Inventing new formats and event types that position the organization as a place of originality
Serving as a compelling public spokesperson on arts, ideas, Jewish programming, community connection, and live events
Maintaining strong relationships with leaders in local and national Jewish communities, arts and culture institutions, and local DC stakeholders
Operating hands-on in a small organization while building on existing strong systems and structure

Qualifications

Proven senior leadership experience with a demonstrated passion for mentoring teams and cultivating strong, diverse team cultures
Experience in arts and culture, live events, media, or mission-driven organizations especially welcome
Deep fluency with Jewish life, culture, and the contemporary Jewish communal landscape
Track record of generating earned income and securing philanthropic support, with entrepreneurial instinct to build new revenue channels
Exceptional written and oral communication and interpersonal skills; a compelling organizational spokesperson
Outstanding emotional intelligence with the ability to navigate politically and culturally sensitive conversations with tact and care
A bridge-builder across diverse constituencies spanning age, education, socioeconomic, religious, and political backgrounds
Leads with kindness, humility, honesty, and humor
Entrepreneurial and experimental, energized by invention and willing to try things that may not work
Demonstrated commitment to equity, inclusion, and belonging
Mission-driven with deep appreciation for the intersection of culture, spirituality, and community life

Additional Details

Benefits include health, vision, and dental insurance, paid vacation and sick leave, retirement savings with matching, pre-tax transit benefit, paid holidays, parental leave, summer Fridays, and office closures during the week of July 4th, between Christmas and New Year's, and select Jewish holidays.

To Apply

Visit Mediabistro for full details and application instructions.

new_jobs.title: Director of Development
new_jobs.description:
Connectly Recruiting has been exclusively engaged by the Louisiana Wildlife and Fisheries Foundation (LAWFF) to identify their inaugural Director of Development. This is a first-of-its-kind role at a 20-year-old conservation foundation — and a rare opportunity to build something from the ground up.

LAWFF is the private fundraising arm of the Louisiana Department of Wildlife and Fisheries. Its programs connect people to Louisiana's natural resources through youth archery, hunter education, whooping crane reintroduction, habitat conservation, and more. The Foundation is now at an inflection point — actively building its strategic vision, rebranding, and ready to unlock a level of corporate and major-gift fundraising it has never pursued at scale.

Louisiana's energy, industrial, and corporate communities represent an enormous, largely untapped pool of conservation-minded sponsors. The right person can unlock that potential — and put LAWFF on the map in a way it has never been before.

The ideal candidate will reside in Louisiana, or a neighboring state (MS, TX, AR) with ability to travel extensively meeting with donors and prospects statewide and regionally.

WHAT YOU'L
L DO

Build LAWFF's development function from the ground up — strategy, structure, and execution
Cultivate and close major gifts and corporate sponsorships across Louisiana and beyond
Serve as LAWFF's chief ambassador, representing the Foundation to donors, partners, and the public
Partner with board members on donor cultivation, introductions, andasks
Expand LAWFF's visibility across Louisiana's conservation, corporate, and philanthropic communities
Travel extensively (50-75%) to build relationships statewide and regionally

WHAT YOU BRING

4+ years in fundraising or development with demonstrated major gifts or corporate sponsorship success
A proven ability to build donor relationships from scratch and close significant asks
A self-starter mindset — you own your pipeline, your calendar, and your results
Genuine connection to hunting, fishing, conservation, or outdoor recreation culture
Executive presence and strong communication skills, written and verbal
Louisiana market knowledge preferred; willingness to be based in or travel regularly to Louisiana required

WHY THIS ROLE

High autonomy — the board is engaged and supportive, not micromanaging
Real access — board members with direct corporate relationships ready to open doors
Meaningful mission — LAWFF's work touches every corner of Louisiana's natural heritage
Growth trajectory — as the Foundation grows, this role grows with it

This is an overview drafted by Connectly Recruiting. Official job description will be provided.

new_jobs.title: President & Chief Executive Officer
new_jobs.description:
Why Seamen’s Bank, and Why Now?

Headquartered on the Outer Cape in Provincetown, Massachusetts and with approximately $500M in assets, Seamen’s Bank just celebrated its 175 th birthday. While we honor our past that is rich with history and success, we now look to our future with confidence and ambition. We are focused on strategically advancing our financial performance, widening and strengthening our customer base, and increasing our community impact.

Seamen’s Bank is seeking an experienced financial executive for the position of President & CEO to propel the Bank into its next stage of growth and development. This top executive will be responsible for the Bank’s overall strategic direction, financial performance, and regulatory compliance. As Seamen’s Bank is a community bank, the incoming leader will need to carefully balance maximizing profitability and asset growth with maintaining deep local relationships and serving the economic needs of the community.

MAJOR RESPONSIBILITIES & ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS

Leadership, Strategic Vision, & Execution:
In collaboration with the Board and the senior management team, develops and executes the long-term vision for the Bank and effectively communicates the vision and strategic planning throughout the Bank.
Oversee the development and implementation of the Bank’s strategic plans and objectives to ensure financial growth and stability.
Brings a thoughtful and common-sense approach to the goals and priorities of the Bank while adhering to the Bank’s mission and values.
Guides and supports the management team in operational decisions and policy development; active on various internal and external committees.
Directly manages, supports, and evaluates the performance of the senior management team.
Able to anticipate and identify developing issues facing the Bank. Proactively and strategically plans to address these issues.
Serves as a member of the Board of Trustees and is the chief liaison between the Board and members of management.
Drive and encourage discussions on decision-making surrounding policies that are established in the best long-term interests of the depositors, staff, and community.
Ability to make critical, difficult, and timely decisions, then execute and follow through to completion and delivery.
Demonstrated ability to shape and steer positive organizational culture and staff engagement.
Upholding an environment that encourages open communication, transparency, and a healthy debate of ideas. Champions change and innovation without instilling fear.

Business Performance & Operations:
The President & CEO will partner with senior management to lead and develop an organization of 75 staff to achieve strategic financial and operational goals. This future leader will ensure the smooth running of the Bank, eliminate ineffective practices, and embrace innovation and forward thinking.
Fosters a culture of excellence, ensuring strong business performance based on innovation, minimizing duplication and waste, and maximizing efficiencies.
Monitors balance sheet strategies, investment portfolio, and capital portfolio and expenditures.
Provides exemplary stewardship of Bank resources.
Demonstrates strong familiarity with technologies to support business and financial functions. 
Monitors overall financial performance and oversees decisions to drive profitability and resource allocation to steer the Bank towards its goals.
Continual review and improvement of all Bank operations, including lending, deposits, credit, other financial services, safety, and security.

Community Relations & Development:
Seamen’s Bank is a solid, long-time financial partner to Outer Cape organizations and depositors. The Bank is a leader largely due to its continued dedication to collaboration and to making impactful improvements to the local economy. To this end, the President & CEO must be both an engaging executive and an inspiring community leader.
Will serve as the primary community ambassador of the Bank with a history of community engagement.
Leadership and/or guidance on the activities of the Seamen’s Bank charitable foundation.
Agile in building and strengthening relationships with customers, business leaders, government officials, and community organizations to improve the Bank’s reputation and establish a wider market presence.
Is an articulate and visible public speaker who can communicate with a diverse audience on a broad range of pressing local issues.
A genuine interest in impacting the local economy.

Risk Identification & Mitigation:
Seamen’s Bank strives for the highest level of compliance and thoughtfully accepts a level of risk aligned with the best interest of the depositors.
A required aptitude for risk management and identifying potential issues at the Bank, within the industry, and potential impact on the community. Develops strategies and initiatives to mitigate those risks. 
Maintains an extensive, comprehensive understanding of state and federal banking laws and regulatory requirements to successfully and effectively steer the Bank through regulatory examinations and audits.
Ensures the Bank complies with federal, state, and local regulations, maintaining ethical standards and risk management practices.
Monitors all activities of the Bank including new loan requests, overdrafts, and exceptions that elevate financial risk.
Leads the Bank through financial or operational challenges, ensuring calm, effective decision-making and clear and transparent communication during crises.
Engages complex Bank matters to legal counsel and ensures best outcomes for the Bank.

ADDITIONAL RESPONSIBILITIES

Regularly communicates, advises, and collaborates with the Board regarding strategic direction, ongoing operations state, arising issues, and policy.
Facilitates cross-departmental efforts to maximize efficiencies and eliminates duplication and waste.
Serves as lead for required compliance exams and various audits.
Sets the standard for professionalism and is a role model, striving toward a culture of excellence. Inspires others to do the same.

Qualifications, Requirements, and Preferred Experience and Skills

Proven track record of excellence as an executive or senior management team member, along with strong business, financial, and regulatory expertise.
Strong leadership, problem-solving, and organizational skills, with the ability to communicate effectively with staff, board members, customers, and the public. Highly skilled at managing relationships at every level and must use sound judgement with the best interests of the Bank at the forefront.
High level of integrity, professionalism, and an unwavering commitment to high ethical standards.
Excel in open communication and can appreciate the Bank’s current state and propel it to the next level.
Experience developing and managing teams.
Minimum of 10-15+ years of progressive experience in the banking or financial services industry, with a proven ability to develop teams and accelerate goals.
Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Business Administration, Economics, or a closely related field.
Must have a valid US issued driver's license.

Physical Demands

The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met to successfully perform the essential functions and duties of this role. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.

While performing the duties of this position, an employee must have the ability to sit at a computer for long periods of time. Specific vision abilities required by this job will include close vision. Extended working hours are expected at a position of this caliber, thus, the ability and flexibility to work consecutive extended days is required.

The employee must occasionally lift and/or move up to 25 pounds. Long periods of standing and moving through community buildings are expected.

Ideal candidates will have confirmed housing on the Mid to Outer Cape.

This job description is not intended to be all-inclusive. The duties and daily activities of the President & CEO will vary, and priorities will quickly shift, depending on the needs to the Bank. Candidates should have a high tolerance for pressurized situations and thrive in a fast-past environment that is both challenging and rewarding.

Salary commensurate to experience.
Pay range: $295,000-$395,000 annually

new_jobs.title: Chief Executive Officer
new_jobs.description:
Confidential Private Equity-Backed Specialty Healthcare GPO

Remote | Executive Leadership

About The Company

Our client is a market-leading specialty healthcare Group Purchasing Organization (GPO) serving independent physician practices across the United States. The organization partners with providers, pharmaceutical manufacturers, and specialty distributors to create purchasing efficiencies for high-cost specialty medications while delivering value to both its physician members and industry partners.

Following a recent investment from a leading private equity firm, the company is entering an exciting new phase of growth. Already profitable and recognized as a leader within its niche, the business is seeking an entrepreneurial Chief Executive Officer to professionalize the organization, expand its commercial capabilities, and execute an ambitious growth strategy.

The Opportunity

This is a unique opportunity to lead a highly differentiated, asset-light healthcare services business at a pivotal stage in its evolution.

Reporting directly to the Board of Directors and private equity sponsor, the CEO will oversee the company's strategic direction while building the infrastructure, leadership team, and commercial organization necessary to accelerate growth. This executive will serve as the face of the organization, strengthening relationships with pharmaceutical manufacturers, specialty distributors, and physician practices while driving long-term enterprise value.

This role is ideally suited for a commercially oriented executive who understands the specialty healthcare GPO landscape and has experience negotiating strategic partnerships, leading complex contracting initiatives, and scaling organizations.

Key Responsibilities

Lead the overall strategy and execution of the company's value creation plan.
Partner closely with the Board and private equity sponsor to execute the long-term growth strategy.
Build and professionalize the organization while maintaining its entrepreneurial culture.
Develop executive relationships with pharmaceutical manufacturers, specialty distributors, and strategic partners.
Lead commercial contracting efforts and negotiate high-impact agreements.
Drive growth of the provider network through strategic membership expansion initiatives.
Recruit, develop, and lead a high-performing team.
Establish scalable operating processes, performance metrics, and organizational infrastructure.
Serve as the company's primary external representative across customers, suppliers, and industry stakeholders.

Ideal Background

Successful candidates will bring executive leadership experience within the specialty healthcare ecosystem, with a strong understanding of pharmaceutical contracting, provider purchasing, and commercial partnership development.

Candidates with direct experience leading or working within a specialty healthcare Group Purchasing Organization (GPO) are particularly encouraged to apply.

Introduction

Executives thrive with us! HCA Healthcare is one of the nation’s leading providers of healthcare services, comprising of over 180 hospitals and about 2,000 sites of care in 21 states and the United Kingdom. We are looking for a Chief Operating Officer for our HCA Houston Healthcare Southeast team where excellence creates excellence.

Benefits

HCA Houston Healthcare Southeast, offers a total rewards package that supports the health, life, career and retirement of our colleagues. The available plans and programs include:

Comprehensive medical coverage that covers many common services at no cost or for a low copay. Plans include prescription drug and behavioral health coverage as well as free telemedicine services and free AirMed medical transportation.
Additional options for dental and vision benefits, life and disability coverage, flexible spending accounts, supplemental health protection plans (accident, critical illness, hospital indemnity), auto and home insurance, identity theft protection, legal counseling, long-term care coverage, moving assistance, pet insurance and more.
Free counseling services and resources for emotional, physical and financial wellbeing
401(k) Plan with a 100% match on 3% to 9% of pay (based on years of service)
Employee Stock Purchase Plan with 10% off HCA Healthcare stock
Family support through fertility and family building benefits with Progyny and adoption assistance.
Referral services for child, elder and pet care, home and auto repair, event planning and more
Consumer discounts through Abenity and Consumer Discounts
Retirement readiness, rollover assistance services and preferred banking partnerships
Education assistance (tuition, student loan, certification support, dependent scholarships)
Colleague recognition program
Time Away From Work Program (paid time off, paid family leave, long- and short-term disability coverage and leaves of absence)
Employee Health Assistance Fund that offers free employee-only coverage to full-time and part-time colleagues based on income.

Learn More About Employee Benefits

Note: Eligibility for benefits may vary by location.

HCA Healthcare has expanded our influence across the healthcare industry by investing $3.5 billion in capital improvements in recent years. Do you want to be an influencer in healthcare? Apply for our Chief Operating Officer role today!

Job Summary And Qualifications

The role of the Chief Operating Officer (COO) assumes line responsibility and authority for the administrative direction, evaluation, and coordination of the functions and activities of assigned departments within the hospital organization to ensure operation objectives and results are in accord with overall hospital needs. In the absence of the hospital, CEO and/or as assigned, represents the CEO in the coordination of entire portions of the hospital organization, speaking and acting within the scope of objectives set forth in the practice and/or policy of the hospital.

What you will do in this role:

You will function at an executive level in an active leadership role with the hospital’s governing body, medical staff leadership, hospital senior leadership and management team
You will be directly responsible for the improvement of the hospitals facilities in assigned areas, including construction or renovation of structures and the purchasing of new equipment
You will be responsible for department leaders in establishing a measure of performance, increase productivity, quality improvement, cost controls and efficient utilization of facilities
You will ensure the consistent and effective execution of key systems and processes that make effective use of organizational resources
You will lead a team or unit to enhance product or service quality; driving the business toward enhanced product or service quality
You will create a work environment in which employees committed to their organization and feel pride and job ownership
You will build strategic alliances and partnerships within the organization to collaboratively execute business strategies
You will create an environment in which products and processes are designed to ensure customer satisfaction; effectively incorporating customer perspectives in all business activities
You will contribute to the development of the organization’s strategic goals and objectives as well as the overall management of the organization
You will promote consistent positive patient interactions that advance the agenda of unparalleled patient service
You will perform other duties as assigned

What qualifications you will need:

Master’s degree in Business Administration, Healthcare Administration, or related field required
3+ years of experience in progressive acute care management and refined management skills as a COO or through a COO development program or similar executive level experience required

HCA Houston Healthcare Southeast
is a 391 - bed acute care facility that has been serving the needs of southeast Harris County for over 45 years. With over 250 physicians on staff specializing in all areas of medicine, and over 1000 employees working to provide the highest level of care the hospital has retained its community hospital atmosphere while attaining state of the art technology.

HCA Healthcare (Corporate)
, based in Nashville, Tennessee, supports a variety of corporate roles from business operations to administrative positions. Like our colleagues in any HCA Healthcare hospital, our corporate campus employees enjoy unparalleled resources and opportunities
to reach their potential as healthcare leaders and innovators. From market rate compensation to continuing education and career advancement opportunities
, every person has a solid foundation for success. Nashville is also home to our Executive Development Program
, where exceptional employees are groomed to take on CNO- and COO-level roles in our hospitals. This selective program focuses on ethics, leadership and the financial and clinical knowledge required of professionals at this level of the industry.

HCA Healthcare has been recognized as one of the World's Most Ethical Companies® by the Ethisphere Institute more than ten times. In recent years, HCA Healthcare spent an estimated $3.7 billion in cost for the delivery of charitable care, uninsured discounts, and other uncompensated expenses.

"Bricks and mortar do not make a hospital. People do."- Dr. Thomas Frist, Sr.

HCA Healthcare Co-Founder

If you're looking for a leadership opportunity that provides both personal satisfaction and professional growth, apply to join HCA Healthcare as a Chief Operating Officer. Unlock your leadership potential with HCA Healthcare.

new_jobs.description:
The Associate Director of Corporate Work Study Program (CWSP) Operations manages the day-to-day operations of CWSP. The Associate Director is responsible for student training and partner service delivery to ensure continuous improvement of the program, and recognition as a quality brand within the San Diego area business and civic community. The Associate Director of CWSP Operations is responsible for communicating with students and their families, and delivering reliable, budget-friendly operational assistance to Cristo Rey San Diego High School. This individual will work closely with and is supervised by the Vice President of Corporate Work Study Program as well as collaborate with the Cristo Rey San Diego High School senior leadership team on a wide variety of organizational decisions. This position is a full time annual position. The Associate Director of CWSP Operations reports directly to the Vice President of Corporate Work Study Program.

Roles and Responsibilities

Essential Client/Student Functions
Day-to-Day CWSP Operations & DOL Compliance
Responsible for overseeing the scheduling of safe and timely transportation of student workers to client sites
Accountable for maintaining compliance with all legal, safety and employment requirements (Federal, State, and local) for the work study program, as well as employment agreements with clients;
Responsible for planning and execution of CWSP pre-matriculation training for incoming students including leading Summer School Academy for CWSP
Responsible for creating a professional curriculum framework that emphasizes workplace skill development (technical and noncognitive) to ensure student employability and success at work
Supervises the effective client placement of student workers and associated work schedules
Makes certain all I-9 and required student worker documentation is provided in a timely manner
Coordinates semi-annual student worker performance evaluations and reporting to Network
Manages the logistics of morning send-off and student return to school
Demonstrates Salesforce expertise in documenting and supporting contract entry, through the SAPR process
Leads and coordinates all CWSP sponsored event development, execution and follow-up.
Responsible for helping to manage the CWSP budget
Collaborates with school departments on any school lead events needing CWSP involvement. Examples include,
Principal and school faculty to deepen curricular connections between what students learn in the classroom, and in the workplace, improving performance in both settings
Director of Admissions to develop / update CWSP new student requirements. Promote and answer questions regarding the CWSP experience for prospective students and their families, as well as conduct non-academic based assessments regarding students’ employability within CWSP
Participates in School leadership teams in developing and enforcing policies and structures to ensure a high level of student performance, programmatic quality control, and healthy client relationships
Participates in School Study Team and Grade Level Team decisions regarding students.

CWSP Communications
Responsible for cultivating a customer relationship management approach to client service activities that emphasizes responsiveness and accuracy
Oversees, in collaboration with CWSP staff, regular and timely communication and resources to support student workers and clients
Drafts and distributes CWSP newsletter stories for distribution to families and/or school distribution
Works collaboratively and enthusiastically as a part of the CWSP team to benefit student workplace and career success
Responsible for the general morale of CWSP staff, providing a quality work environment through teaming principles, effective leadership and a demonstrated positive attitude
Supports engagement with the Cristo Rey Network, including participation at CWSP-specific gatherings, professional development and the Annual Network Meeting and responding to routine and periodic requests for program specific data from the Network

Transportation Management
Provide oversight and leadership for the Transportation Manager, ensuring alignment with organizational objectives
Responsible for the management and oversight of the following in conjunction with the Transportation Manager
Oversee auto-van safety protocols, ensuring adherence to industry best practices and regulatory standards
Monitor legal compliance of transportation routes, making necessary adjustments to align with requirements
Develop, implement, and refine van check-out systems to improve efficiency and accountability
Manage van maintenance schedules, coordinating routine servicing and addressing mechanical concerns proactively
Assess and monitor driver proficiency, providing feedback and performance evaluations to maintain operational excellence
Design and deliver professional development topics and instructional sessions on a quarterly basis for drivers
Supervise and evaluate drivers, implementing corrective actions and support strategies as needed

Remote Work Management
Management of and supervisory role for students working in a remote worksite
Support Associate Directors, Partner Relations team with the management of partners with remote work study programs

Professional Presence
Model values consistent with Catholic education and service to others
Approach learning through a developmental lens, and enthusiastically participate in professional development; seek and apply feedback from the instructional leadership and from colleagues; demonstrate drive and independence in accessing resources to further your own improvement
Contribute fully, intentionally, and constructively to the team in a range of ways: analyze student data, identify students in need of additional support, problem-solve unanticipated challenges that arise within the school
Collaborate with peers with integrity, humility, commitment, humor, and joy to ensure the foundation of trust that will enable the team to accomplish its ambitious goals
Engage students and parents with respect, fairness, and empathy to enable full partnership with the school
Ability to work appropriately with High School age students, minors and to build rapport with this age group as designated in conduct required in the School Handbook.

Skill and ability to:
Resolve and report route issues between drivers and partners; other customer service issues
Supervise and motivate employees in a team-based environment
Interpret transportation operations anomalies and identify appropriate solutions
Prepare administrative and technical documents and reports
Work effectively in a general business environment, with a focus on high levels of quality and customer service
Fluent in English and Spanish (preferred; not required)
Ability to independently work effectively and efficiently, as well as within a team structure.
Attends professional development to enhance employment

General Administrative/ Other Responsibilities
Return voice-mail/e-mail messages in a timely manner
Communicate regularly with parents
Demonstrate a respectful demeanor with staff, students, and parents
Observe utmost confidentiality regarding parent, student, staff information/conversations
Supervise campus and other areas of duty as the role may require
Other duties as assigned

Bachelor’s degree (preferred)
Clear criminal background check (a clear Livescan is a condition of employment)
Record of strong personability with clients
Demonstrates positive characteristics in working with clients
Demonstrates experience and ability to build rapport with high school students
Proficiency using technology to advance teaching and learning
Proven background of analyzing and utilizing multiple sources of data to increase student achievement
Demonstrates strong organizational and presentation skills
Understands how to develop curriculum to engage high school students and support technical and personable work study skills
Excellent verbal and written communication skills required
Background in managing personnel in the Van Driver fleet
Relentless focus on results, high level of accountability, and willingness to do “whatever it takes”
Demonstrated commitment to the mission of the Cristo Rey Network and model
Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, entrepreneurial school environment
A belief that all students can and will succeed in college and career
Experience working with English learners is a plus
Bilingual in Spanish is a plus
Positive professional references and recommendations

Respects and understands a Catholic school philosophy

Join us in Telling the Stories of Santa’s North Pole!

The Lumistella Company is a global intellectual property company dedicated to bringing Santa’s North Pole to life for children around the world through consumer products, original entertainment and brand experiences. We are home to the most iconic and beloved Christmas brands of this generation including The Elf on the Shelf, Elf Pets, and Elf Mates.

The Executive Director, Experiences & Activations
leads the planning, coordination, and execution of Lumistella's experience- and activation-based commercialization opportunities. This role serves as the central business owner responsible for guiding opportunities from concept through delivery while ensuring alignment with franchise strategy, commercial objectives, creative vision, and brand standards.

Working across the enterprise, the Executive Director brings together internal stakeholders and external partners to create engaging, high-quality experiences that deepen consumer participation, strengthen partner relationships, and advance Lumistella's Franchise Commercialization strategy.

The Executive Director, Experiences & Activations will:
Commercial Opportunity Leadership

Serve as the business owner for assigned experience and activation opportunities from initial concept through execution and post-project evaluation.
Shepherd opportunities through the commercialization lifecycle by establishing clear objectives, coordinating stakeholders, managing timelines, resolving issues, and maintaining forward momentum.
Evaluate new opportunities, recommend appropriate paths forward, and ensure alignment with Lumistella's strategic priorities and commercial objectives.
Balance multiple concurrent initiatives while maintaining visibility, accountability, and communication across the organization.

Experiences & Activations Portfolio Management

Lead Lumistella's portfolio of consumer experiences, franchise activations, corporate events, employee events, executive events, trade shows, and other strategic engagement opportunities.
Oversee planning, budgeting, scheduling, partner coordination, and execution to ensure successful delivery of each initiative.
Continuously evaluate opportunities to improve the quality, consistency, organization, and impact of Lumistella's experiences and activations.
Maintain an integrated view of the portfolio to proactively manage priorities, dependencies, and resource requirements.

Cross-Functional Leadership & Collaboration

Serve as the primary point of coordination across internal business functions throughout the planning and delivery of experiences and activations.
Build strong partnerships with Creative, Story, Consumer Products, Marketing, Franchise Development, Legal, Finance, Operations, Philanthropy, Executive Leadership, and other key stakeholders.
Facilitate decision-making, resolve cross-functional issues, and ensure organizational alignment throughout the lifecycle of each opportunity.
Foster a collaborative working environment that enables teams to deliver integrated franchise experiences.

Experience Development & Story Integration

Partner closely with Creative, Art, Brand, and Story teams to develop engaging, high-quality franchise experiences.
Ensure storytelling is thoughtfully integrated throughout the guest journey by identifying key experience moments where narrative, character engagement, and emotional connection enhance participation and strengthen the franchise.
Provide creative leadership throughout concept development, experience design, creative review, refinement, and execution while respecting established creative governance and approval processes.
Develop creative briefs and collaborate with internal creative teams to ensure experiences align with franchise strategy, brand standards, and commercial objectives.

Partner & Account Management

Develop and maintain strong relationships with strategic partners, agencies, production partners, venues, vendors, licensees, and other organizations supporting Lumistella's Experiences & Activations portfolio.
Serve as the primary business contact for assigned accounts and partners, ensuring effective communication, accountability, and successful collaboration.
Represent Lumistella with professionalism while protecting the company's brand standards, strategic interests, and commercial objectives.

Operational Excellence

Lead the day-to-day management of assigned initiatives through disciplined planning, budgeting, scheduling, communication, documentation, and risk management.
Improve visibility, organization, reporting, and execution across the Experiences & Activations portfolio.
Establish practical tools, processes, and operating disciplines that improve consistency and support future organizational growth.
Monitor project performance and identify opportunities for continuous improvement.

If you want to be one of us...

The ideal candidate will bring:
Experience leading complex projects involving multiple internal stakeholders and external partners.
Exceptional organizational, project management, and relationship management skills.
Strong commercial judgment and business acumen.
Experience collaborating with creative teams throughout concept development and execution.
Ability to influence and lead without direct authority.
Experience managing multiple priorities in a fast-paced, entrepreneurial environment.
Strong communication and executive presentation skills.
Experience managing budgets, contracts, vendors, and external partnerships.
Experience working within an intellectual property, entertainment, franchise, or consumer products environment is highly desirable.
Protects company assets through the proper management of asset files and adherence to company policy with regard to information security and management.
This position is based in Atlanta, Georgia, and does not include relocation assistance. Candidates must be able to relocate at their own expense if selected.

Assistant Director of Development, Corporate Relations- College of Nursing

Job Description

The Assistant Director of Development, Corporate Relations serves as a strategic leader responsible for cultivating, securing, and stewarding corporate partnerships that advance the mission and priorities of the College of Nursing. This role focuses on building meaningful collaborations that support academic programs, research initiatives, workforce development, scholarships, and community engagement. Reporting to the College’s Senior Director of Advancement, the position drives impactful philanthropic and programmatic investments.

Portfolio Management

Build and manage a portfolio of corporate prospects and partners of all capacities with a focus on proposals $25,000 and above.
Identify, cultivate, solicit and steward corporate prospects that align with the strategic priorities of the College of Nursing.
Assist in developing and executing tailored engagement strategies to secure funding for scholarships, academic programs, research, and capital initiatives.
Conduct research and make independent judgments regarding corporate prospects and industry partners to determine potential alignment and engagement strategies.
Prepare compelling proposals, presentations, and sponsorship packages that effectively communicate institutional priorities and partnership opportunities.
Provide guidance to university personnel regarding corporate funding sources, prospect research, proposal preparation, and solicitation, when appropriate.
Coordinate internal conference calls with Sponsored Research staff and other interested parties to discuss and develop strategies for major foundation-issued RFPs.
Collaborate where appropriate with other Corporate and Foundation Relations staff members across UF Foundation to coordinate College involvement with multi-unit prospects.
Maintain accurate records of corporate interactions, proposals, and gifts in accordance with university policies and procedures
Prioritize the dean’s development efforts and optimize travel schedule.

Program Management

Identify and cultivate relationships with corporate partners to advance the College’s mission and strategic initiatives.
Build a portfolio of fundraising prospects and strategically manage relationships utilizing a strategic moves management approach to yield maximum philanthropic support.
Actively monitor proposal progression, including processing letters of agreement, tracking proposals, meeting reporting deadlines, and responding to requests for additional information.
Facilitate corporate-sponsored programs such as internships, workforce development initiatives, continuing education opportunities, and research collaborations.
Properly handle leadership, annual gifts and pledges made to the University.
Determine fundraising and alumni policies as they pertain to the development.
Coordinate and oversee campus visits for corporate representatives, engaging key stakeholders, building itineraries, and designing customized experiences.

Strategic Initiatives

Serve as the primary liaison between the College of Nursing and corporate partners.
Establish and maintain strong relationships with healthcare organizations, corporations, and industry leaders.
Collaborate with development officers, faculty, and administrators to identify partnership opportunities that align with corporate interests.
Work closely with faculty and researchers to connect corporate partners with initiatives that support innovation and workforce needs.
Meet regularly with Corporate & Foundation Relations colleagues across UF Advancement to foster collaboration and ensure alignment with university-wide corporate strategy.
Develop and communicate best practices and offer targeted training to support growing corporate and foundation relationships.
Represent the College of Nursing at corporate meetings, industry events, and professional conferences.

Expected Salary

$53,000-$60,000

Required Qualifications

Master's degree in an appropriate area of specialization; or a bachelor's degree in an appropriate area of specialization and one year of relevant experience

Preferred

The ideal candidate will possess:

One to three years of major gift fundraising experience in corporate and foundation relations is strongly preferred.
Prior experience in a university or nonprofit environment.
General knowledge and understanding of business/economic environment.
Knowledge of fundraising principles, methods, and standards.
Demonstrated successful experience in prospect identification, relationship building, solicitation and stewardship.
The ability to work strategically, develop and implement constituency programs and activities, along with a talent for motivating volunteers.
Ability to work collaboratively and a team player.
Competency in problem solving.
Ability to manage multiple projects and meet deadlines.

new_jobs.title: Business Development Director, Government
new_jobs.description:
The Opportunity

Solarcore is an advanced materials technology company that develops the most scientifically innovative thermal solutions on the planet. We are on a mission to solve the world’s largest thermal efficiency problems by revolutionizing the antiquated world of thermal insulation.

Solarcore is seeking a Business Development Director, Government to focus on thermal insulation applications across the U.S. Department of War and allied nations. This is a high-impact, strategic role responsible for shaping long-term growth, driving major captures, and positioning Solarcore as the premier thermal insulation provider to the Defense Industry. The ideal candidate brings deep domain expertise, executive-level customer relationships, and a proven record of winning complex, mission-critical programs.

The Business Development Director, Government will be engaging with the U.S. Department of War, allied nations, Government Prime Contractors & key government stakeholders.
We are looking for a dynamic, credible, and disciplined leader who can clearly communicate complex ideas without unnecessary complexity and build trust with senior government, military, and industry counterparts.

Key Responsibilities

Serve as a senior growth leader for thermal insulation within the Defense Market, helping shape and executing market and capture strategies
Build, maintain, and expand executive-level relationships across US DoW operational, technical, and acquisition communities
Identify, qualify, and mature a robust portfolio of US DoW thermal insulation opportunities supporting near-, mid-, and long-term growth
Lead and oversee capture strategies for priority pursuits, ensuring disciplined execution across the full BD lifecycle
Partner with Government Prime leadership, capture executives, program managers, and solution architects to align investments, offerings, and messaging
Drive competitive positioning and differentiation through deep understanding of customer needs, mission priorities, and competitor capabilities
Coordinate across Solarcore business areas to deliver integrated, enterprise-level solutions to customer challenges
Assist in delivering annual bookings, revenue, pipeline, and award targets with Solarcore Sales team
Guide development of marketing collateral, white papers, and thought leadership that highlight Solarcore’s capabilities
Represent Solarcore at industry forums, tradeshows, customer engagements, strategic planning events and company meetings at Solarcore HQ in Portland, OR. Travel expectations 50%+
Support company compliance efforts by preparing compliance matrices for proposal development and maintain evidence of conformity for government audits
Collaborate with product and technical team at Solarcore to translate complex customer requirements into compliant material specifications and validation metrics
Manage compliance with ITAR (International Traffic in Arms Regulations), EAR (Export Administration Regulations), and U.S. Customs requirements for all cross-border material movements
Maintain thorough understanding and enforcement of FAR (Federal Acquisition Regulation) and DFARS (Defense Federal Acquisition Regulation Supplement) guidelines
Other duties as assigned

Experience and Requirements

Ability to obtain a Top Secret clearance; active clearance strongly preferred.
Deep understanding of government (including DoW) acquisition processes, contracting, customer buying behaviors and compliance is required.
Ideally, this person has led business development or capture efforts in defense, particularly within thermal insulation, soft goods, hard‑tech, and/or advanced manufacturing environments.
Proven success leading and winning large, complex defense programs.
Established senior-level relationships with DoW & allied nation stakeholders.
Ability to lead and influence across organizational and geographic boundaries.
Exceptional written and verbal communication skills, including executive and flag-level briefings.
Proficient in ITAR, EAR, FAR, DFARS, and chemical/material compliance frameworks (REACH, Prop 65)
Professional credentials such as a Certified U.S. Export Compliance Officer (CUSECO) or similar designations a plus

Our Vision

Create Thermal Technology That Empowers Humanity to Reach Our Full Potential

The Culture

Solarcore’s continued success is made possible by the Solarcore team. The Solarcore team is made up of hardworking individuals that live and breathe Solarcore’s Pillars—
Have Grit
Be Adaptable
Take Ownership
Be Conscious
Be Transformative
Be Collaborative

These values are the common thread between every Solarcore team member, and they ultimately provide the foundation for our success.

Director of Advancement

Houston, TX

Our client, a well-established Catholic preparatory high school in Houston, is seeking an experienced, relationship-driven Director of Advancement to lead the school's fundraising, donor engagement, and advancement strategy. We need a strategic fundraising leader with proven ability to cultivate major donors, strengthen annual giving, and develop innovative philanthropic initiatives. new_jobs.title: Managing Director, Security Principal
new_jobs.description:
Founded in 1988, Trimont (www.trimont.com) is a specialized global commercial real estate loan services provider and partner for lenders and investors seeking the infrastructure and capabilities needed to help them scale their business and make informed, effective decisions related to the deployment, management and administration of commercial real estate secured credit. As the largest master servicer of commercial real estate loans in the United States, Trimont manages securitizations with meticulous oversight and coordination—protecting cash flows, mitigating risk, and ensuring portfolio performance.

We do this with a team of 1100+ extraordinary team members who serve a global client base from offices in Atlanta (headquarters), Bengaluru, Charlotte, Dallas, Hyderabad, Kansas City, London, New York and Sydney. We empower our people with advanced technology, industry-leading knowledge, and a culture centered on our core values. This approach enables our teams to deliver exceptional client service, build lasting relationships and take pride in the high-quality work they perform.

Trimont is an innovative firm where visionary professionals come to learn, grow, and thrive with colleagues driven by curiosity and collaboration.

Learn:
We believe ongoing learning is critical and are focused on providing a work environment where all team members can take ownership of their careers.

Grow:
We work alongside the largest institutional lenders in the world, overseeing the most significant projects in the industry. This unique opportunity allows us to broaden our skillset and develop our abilities by tackling some of the industry's most challenging and exciting endeavors.

Thrive:
Our firm is a place where ethics and excellence meet to create an experience that matches our capabilities. There are no limits to what we as team members and as an organization, can achieve together.

Where people, purpose, and progress come together every day.

Job Summary

The Managing Director, Security Principal is Trimont's senior technical leader for security. Reporting to the Chief Security Officer (CSO), this role owns the technical depth behind the security program: architecting solutions, evaluating vendor technology, and building custom tooling, including AI-assisted and agentic automation, where commercial products fall short.

This is a hands-on engineering and architecture role with no direct reports. The Security Principal partners closely with the CSO, Chief Technology Officer (CTO), the Security Operations Center as a Service (SOCaaS) provider, and internal engineering teams to translate strategy into working, defensible systems.

Responsibilities

Serve as the senior technical authority for Trimont’s security program, setting architecture standards and technical direction across identity, network, cloud, application, and data security domains.
Lead build vs. buy decisions by evaluating vendor solutions and designing/building custom integrations or platforms where commercial products do not meet requirements.
Design, build, and operate AI-assisted and agentic automation for security operations, including alert triage, vulnerability management, and compliance evidence collection, with appropriate human oversight.
Own technical architecture and integration strategy for core security platforms (SIEM, IAM/SSO/MFA, DLP, EDR, vulnerability management).
Architect and scale secure software development lifecycle (SDLC) practices by integrating automated security controls into DevOps pipelines.
Act as a senior technical escalation point during security incidents, partnering with SOCaaS and internal teams for detection, investigation, and response.
Lead enterprise vulnerability management, including scanning architecture, penetration test scoping, remediation validation, and prioritization.
Embed security requirements early by conducting architecture and design reviews for major technology initiatives.
Prototype and evaluate emerging technologies (e.g., agentic AI, blockchain security, zero trust), providing recommendations based on hands-on testing.
Contribute hands-on engineering work (code, scripts, playbooks, automation) in addition to technical review and guidance.
Mentor and upskill security and engineering teams as a technical leader and force multiplier, without direct management responsibilities.
Represent Trimont’s technical security posture in client, auditor, and regulator engagements as directed by the CSO.

Required Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in computer science, information security, or related field; equivalent hands-on experience considered.
15+ years in security engineering, architecture, or offensive security, with progression to Staff/Principal-level individual contributor.
Deep, hands-on technical proficiency in at least three of: application security/secure SDLC, cloud security architecture (AWS/Azure/GCP), IAM engineering, network/perimeter security, SIEM/SOAR engineering, or offensive security.
Demonstrated experience building automation or tooling (custom integrations, scripts, orchestration) across security platforms.
Practical experience building AI-assisted or agentic automation (e.g., orchestration frameworks, tool-calling agents, LLM API integration).
At least one hands-on technical certification (e.g., OSCP, OSCE, GPEN, GCIH, GXPN, AWS/Azure security certification).
Working knowledge of regulatory and control frameworks relevant to financial services (NIST, ISO 27001, GLBA, GDPR, CCPA) to engineer compliant controls.
Strong written and verbal communication skills for technical and executive audiences.

Preferred Qualifications

CISSP, CISM, or CISA certifications (for cross-functional credibility with governance stakeholders).
Experience in financial services, commercial real estate, or other highly regulated industries.
Proven development experience

Trimont is an equal opportunity employer, and we’re proud to support and celebrate diversity in the workplace. If you have a disability and need an accommodation or assistance with the application process and/or using our website, please contact us. We are proud to maintain a drug-free policy, ensuring that our community is a secure and productive space for all our team members.

new_jobs.title: Program Director
new_jobs.description:
Reports to:
Executive director

Employment Status:
Full-Time (40 hrs/wk)

Supervises:
8 direct reports (program operations manager, program managers, program coordinators)

CircEsteem is seeking a highly organized, collaborative, and mission-driven Program Director. Our mission is to unite youth across all backgrounds and identities by fostering self-esteem and mutual respect through the practice and performance of circus arts. The Program Director is a senior leadership position overseeing the strategic visioning, implementation, and fidelity of our youth and adult programming.

Rooted in the global tradition of social circus, CircEsteem blends circus arts and social intervention to support whole-person development, social inclusion, artistic expression, and life skills, especially for youth navigating heightened barriers and stressors. CircEsteem is often described as “a place called home” and operates from a dedicated social circus facility that serves as both a training ground and a community anchor.

Position Summary:

CircEsteem’s Program Director is responsible for the overall success and quality of all programming through providing strategy, planning, and implementation; cultivating and strengthening community partnerships; fundraising and grant development, compliance, and outcomes evaluation; curriculum development and fidelity; external public relations; and staff professional development and leadership. This position is a cross-functional position working with our operations director, development, communications, and outreach and partnership managers. This person will also provide leadership to our part-time teaching artist pool who make up most of our teaching staff. The Program Director also provides updates and advises the board of directors on all programmatic matters and leads the board’s programming committee. The person in this role is a thoughtful team leader who thrives in a dynamic, diverse, distributed, youth-serving environment.

The ideal candidate will have:

knowledge of social circus and the circus arts.
knowledge of positive youth development (e.g., SEL) frameworks and assessment tools and their application to youth populations (approx. ages 6-18).
an innovative, visionary, hands-on, humble, and empathetic leadership style.
deep experience in program evaluation and data collection and reporting.
some experience in fundraising and/or grant development, implementation, and reporting.
a professional history that is rooted in diversity and social justice with a passion for uniting youth from diverse backgrounds and making Chicago a better place through youth programming.
supervisory experience with part-time staff, contractors, or teaching artists.
experience utilizing Salesforce as a CRM and data-collection tool.

Duties and Responsibilities:

Program Leadership and Supervision
Lead the strategic visioning, planning, and implementation for all youth programming including: ensuring all program curricula are current and relevant; programs are evaluated appropriately and data reported regularly; staff have necessary knowledge and skills to meet program outcomes; relationships with partner organizations are strengthened and new relationships are formed.
Provide the supervision, professional development, and capacity-building infrastructure for staff reports including part-time teaching artists through bi-annual evaluations, in collaboration with the internal teaching artist professional development cohort, and by cutivating a culture of inclusion and belonging.
Manage the program staff hiring and onboarding process in partnership with the operations director ensuring that all pre-employment requirements, new hire paperwork, training, onboarding documentation, and employment compliance materials are completed accurately and on time.
Create and manage comprehensive onboarding schedules in partnership with relevant team members, including program shadowing, professional development sessions, hands-on training, and other readiness activities required before teaching artists are assigned to programs.

Monitoring and Evaluating
Develop and systematize data tracking systems to measure and report on program outcomes and efficacy to funders, community supporters and collaborative partners (namely through strengthening our Salesforce platform and providing professional development to our programs staff on how to use the system).
Create new opportunities to evaluate our programs.
Maintain data integrity, cleanliness, and ease of use for all members of the staff.
Prepare monthly, quarterly, and annual reports as requested by the development manager and executive director.

Grants and Fundraising
Support grant development including program development, budgeting, evaluation, reporting, and grant writing.
Ensure that all grant requirements are being met (e.g., enrollment, reporting, budgets) with support from the program operations manager and other relevant staff.
Oversee all documents and program audit materials.
Write and submit all final reports for grants as necessary alongside the development manager.
Work with the executive director and development manager to identify funders and steward relationships that support our programs.

Curriculum Development and Fidelity
Lead the uniform creation, development, revisions, updates, and implementation of all CircEsteem curriculum alongside program managers and coordinators.
Create and implement systems for the regular reporting of curriculum implementation to ensure excellence.
Ensure that curriculum meets positive youth development outcomes and other relevant programmatic and grant outcomes.

Administrative
Provide leadership on site-specific budgeting and oversee program expenditures alongside program managers and coordinators.
Regularly inform the executive director of program and project status (finance).
Support the executive director and operations director in writing a comprehensive employee handbook, policies and procedures book, and program handbook.
Additional tasks may vary from time to time.

Public Relations
Provide public presentations and demonstrations as needed, including representing the program team to the board of directors and external partners.
Support executive director in presenting at relevant trade shows, events, volunteer fairs and organizations (as necessary).

Qualifications:

Master’s degree required ideally in education, arts education, youth development, non-profit leadership, or similar.
Minimum of seven years of experience in program development, implementation, and management with a knowledge of Chicago’s arts and neighborhood landscape.
Demonstrated experience in developing and operationalizing a data-driven culture.
Strong written and verbal communication skills.
Strong equity-driven leadership, interpersonal, supervision, administration and management experience.
Ability to handle and prioritize multiple tasks while maintaining attention to detail.
Sound judgement, professionalism, and a collaborative and developmental leadership philosophy.
Ability and willingness to work in a distributed organization structure, including working 4 days a week on-site in Uptown most of the year and 5 days a week during the summer on-site. Comfort working with staff across our Chicago program sites via distance communication tools (e.g., email, text, Google Suite).
